data-turn-id="5432e67c-76fa-4579-862c-c10ef1e95719" data-testid="conversation-turn-1" data-scroll-anchor="false" data-turn="assistant"><h6 class="sr-only">ChatGPT said:</h6><div class="text-base group/turn-messages focus-visible:outline-hidden relative flex w-full min-w-0 flex-col agent-turn" tabindex="-1"><div class="flex max-w-full flex-col grow"><div data-message-author-role="assistant" data-message-id="5432e67c-76fa-4579-862c-c10ef1e95719" dir="auto" class="min-h-8 text-message relative flex w-full flex-col items-end gap-2 text-start break-words whitespace-normal [.text-message+&amp;]:mt-5" data-message-model-slug="gpt-5"><div class="flex w-full flex-col gap-1 empty:hidden first:pt-[3px]"><div class="markdown prose dark:prose-invert w-full break-words dark markdown-new-styling"><p data-start="0" data-end="244">Perfeito, João. Reescrevi o texto incluindo a explicação detalhada do <strong data-start="70" data-end="127">painel visual expansível por capítulos e subcapítulos</strong>, com as cores e a lógica de ativação/descartes conforme o usuário responde. Aqui vai a versão completa atualizada:</p> <hr data-start="246" data-end="249"> <h1 data-start="251" data-end="300">📌 Contexto + MVP – Gerador de Checklists IRC</h1> <p data-start="302" data-end="547">🔹 <strong data-start="305" data-end="329">Como a ideia começou</strong><br data-start="329" data-end="332"> A primeira visão era muito ambiciosa: um programa global de RegTech que usaria IA para interpretar qualquer norma (IRC, IBC, ISO, até regras internas de bancos) e transformar em checklists automáticos e dinâmicos.</p> <p data-start="549" data-end="688">A IA faria tudo: ler o código, extrair regras, criar condicionais, atualizar quando houvesse novas versões, e entregar compliance pronto.</p> <p data-start="690" data-end="736">Discutimos viabilidade técnica e de mercado:</p> <ul data-start="737" data-end="971"> <li data-start="737" data-end="814"> <p data-start="739" data-end="814">É possível tecnicamente (NLP + vetorização + IA para interpretar normas).</p> </li> <li data-start="815" data-end="892"> <p data-start="817" data-end="892">Seria um mercado gigante, mas com risco alto de competição com big techs.</p> </li> <li data-start="893" data-end="971"> <p data-start="895" data-end="971">O caminho mais seguro seria começar nichado (ex.: construção civil / IRC).</p> </li> </ul> <p data-start="973" data-end="1001">🔹 <strong data-start="976" data-end="999">Ajustes do conceito</strong></p> <ul data-start="1002" data-end="1579"> <li data-start="1002" data-end="1071"> <p data-start="1004" data-end="1071">Redefinimos o papel da IA: ela não será o motor final do sistema.</p> </li> <li data-start="1072" data-end="1165"> <p data-start="1074" data-end="1165">Ela servirá apenas como apoio para extrair e resumir normas durante a fase de preparação.</p> </li> <li data-start="1166" data-end="1287"> <p data-start="1168" data-end="1287">A base de dados final (checklists, condições de ativação, perguntas) será criada manualmente por nós e será estática.</p> </li> <li data-start="1288" data-end="1371"> <p data-start="1290" data-end="1371">O app não valida medidas nem cálculos (ex.: “altura do guarda-corpo = 36 in?”).</p> </li> <li data-start="1372" data-end="1497"> <p data-start="1374" data-end="1497">Ele só indica: “porque você marcou que tem basement + dormitórios, deve se atentar a saídas de emergência conforme R310”.</p> </li> <li data-start="1498" data-end="1579"> <p data-start="1500" data-end="1579">A camada final é sempre um checklist de atenção, não uma inspeção automática.</p> </li> </ul> <p data-start="1581" data-end="1628">Decidimos oferecer duas versões do checklist:</p> <ul data-start="1629" data-end="1752"> <li data-start="1629" data-end="1674"> <p data-start="1631" data-end="1674"><strong data-start="1631" data-end="1642">Técnica</strong> (texto fiel/resumido do IRC).</p> </li> <li data-start="1675" data-end="1752"> <p data-start="1677" data-end="1752"><strong data-start="1677" data-end="1686">Leiga</strong> (tradução em linguagem simples, para contratistas ou clientes).</p> </li> </ul> <p data-start="1754" data-end="1864">🔹 <strong data-start="1757" data-end="1779">Problema resolvido</strong><br data-start="1779" data-end="1782"> O IRC tem 1100+ páginas. Se transformado em perguntas diretas, ficaria infinito.</p> <p data-start="1866" data-end="1907">Para resolver, estruturamos em camadas:</p> <ul data-start="1908" data-end="2168"> <li data-start="1908" data-end="2008"> <p data-start="1910" data-end="2008"><strong data-start="1910" data-end="1940">Camada 1 (macroperguntas):</strong> 10–15 perguntas universais que filtram blocos inteiros de normas.</p> </li> <li data-start="2009" data-end="2107"> <p data-start="2011" data-end="2107"><strong data-start="2011" data-end="2041">Camada 2 (intermediárias):</strong> só abrem se relevantes (ex.: se há basement, piscina, garagem).</p> </li> <li data-start="2108" data-end="2168"> <p data-start="2110" data-end="2168"><strong data-start="2110" data-end="2134">Camada 3 (detalhes):</strong> exceções e pontos finos, raros.</p> </li> </ul> <p data-start="2170" data-end="2212">Isso reduz o uso prático para o cliente:</p> <ul data-start="2213" data-end="2311"> <li data-start="2213" data-end="2263"> <p data-start="2215" data-end="2263">Potenciais perguntas no IRC inteiro: ~350–400.</p> </li> <li data-start="2264" data-end="2311"> <p data-start="2266" data-end="2311">Perguntas reais para projeto típico: 20–40.</p> </li> </ul> <p data-start="2313" data-end="2447">🔹 <strong data-start="2316" data-end="2333">Painel Visual</strong><br data-start="2333" data-end="2336"> O sistema terá um <strong data-start="2354" data-end="2382">painel visual expansível</strong>, que lista todos os capítulos e subcapítulos do IRC em árvore.</p> <ul data-start="2448" data-end="2954"> <li data-start="2448" data-end="2500"> <p data-start="2450" data-end="2500">Inicialmente todos aparecem <strong data-start="2478" data-end="2497">cinza (inativo)</strong>.</p> </li> <li data-start="2501" data-end="2830"> <p data-start="2503" data-end="2589">Conforme o cliente preenche as informações, o sistema “ativa” ou descarta cada item:</p> <ul data-start="2592" data-end="2830"> <li data-start="2592" data-end="2658"> <p data-start="2594" data-end="2658"><strong data-start="2594" data-end="2656">Verde = ativado (se aplica ao projeto, precisa de atenção)</strong></p> </li> <li data-start="2661" data-end="2735"> <p data-start="2663" data-end="2735"><strong data-start="2663" data-end="2733">Vermelho = descartado (não se aplica, já filtrado pelas respostas)</strong></p> </li> <li data-start="2738" data-end="2830"> <p data-start="2740" data-end="2830"><strong data-start="2740" data-end="2828">Cinza = ainda inativo (não avaliado porque não foi atingido pelo fluxo de perguntas)</strong></p> </li> </ul> </li> <li data-start="2831" data-end="2954"> <p data-start="2833" data-end="2954">Esse painel é interativo e expansível: o usuário pode abrir capítulos e subcapítulos para ver a situação de cada regra.</p> </li> </ul> <hr data-start="2956" data-end="2959"> <h1 data-start="2961" data-end="2999">🚀 MVP – Gerador de Checklists IRC</h1> <p data-start="3001" data-end="3049">🎯 <strong data-start="3004" data-end="3016">Objetivo</strong><br data-start="3016" data-end="3019"> Criar um aplicativo web que:</p> <ul data-start="3050" data-end="3392"> <li data-start="3050" data-end="3102"> <p data-start="3052" data-end="3102">Faz perguntas macro ao usuário (10–15 iniciais).</p> </li> <li data-start="3103" data-end="3147"> <p data-start="3105" data-end="3147">Ativa ou descarta seções do IRC inteiro.</p> </li> <li data-start="3148" data-end="3241"> <p data-start="3150" data-end="3241">Mostra um painel visual expansível com capítulos e subcapítulos (cinza, verde, vermelho).</p> </li> <li data-start="3242" data-end="3299"> <p data-start="3244" data-end="3299">Gera um checklist final com os requisitos aplicáveis.</p> </li> <li data-start="3300" data-end="3347"> <p data-start="3302" data-end="3347">Mostra versão técnica e leiga de cada item.</p> </li> <li data-start="3348" data-end="3392"> <p data-start="3350" data-end="3392">Permite exportar checklist em PDF/Excel.</p> </li> </ul> <p data-start="3394" data-end="3421">🛠️ <strong data-start="3398" data-end="3419">Estrutura Técnica</strong></p> <ul data-start="3422" data-end="3865"> <li data-start="3422" data-end="3559"> <p data-start="3424" data-end="3492"><strong data-start="3424" data-end="3442">Base de dados:</strong> JSON/Postgres com regras de ativação (if/else).</p> <ul data-start="3495" data-end="3559"> <li data-start="3495" data-end="3559"> <p data-start="3497" data-end="3559">Exemplo: <code data-start="3506" data-end="3541">basement=true AND dormitorio=true</code> ativa <code data-start="3548" data-end="3556">R310.1</code>.</p> </li> </ul> </li> <li data-start="3560" data-end="3657"> <p data-start="3562" data-end="3657"><strong data-start="3562" data-end="3585">Checklist estática:</strong> criada manualmente, validada por nós, sem depender de IA em produção.</p> </li> <li data-start="3658" data-end="3735"> <p data-start="3660" data-end="3735"><strong data-start="3660" data-end="3673">Frontend:</strong> React ou Next.js (formulário adaptativo + painel colorido).</p> </li> <li data-start="3736" data-end="3779"> <p data-start="3738" data-end="3779"><strong data-start="3738" data-end="3750">Backend:</strong> Node.js ou Python simples.</p> </li> <li data-start="3780" data-end="3834"> <p data-start="3782" data-end="3834"><strong data-start="3782" data-end="3797">Hospedagem:</strong> Vercel (frontend) + Supabase (DB).</p> </li> <li data-start="3835" data-end="3865"> <p data-start="3837" data-end="3865"><strong data-start="3837" data-end="3852">Exportação:</strong> PDF/Excel.</p> </li> </ul> <p data-start="3867" data-end="3888">📊 <strong data-start="3870" data-end="3886">Fluxo de Uso</strong></p> <ol data-start="3889" data-end="4299"> <li data-start="3889" data-end="4004"> <p data-start="3892" data-end="4004">Usuário responde perguntas macro (ocupação, fundação, basement, garagem, piscina, condições ambientais, etc.).</p> </li> <li data-start="4005" data-end="4062"> <p data-start="4008" data-end="4062">Sistema marca quais seções do IRC se aplicam ou não.</p> </li> <li data-start="4063" data-end="4137"> <p data-start="4066" data-end="4137">O painel visual mostra capítulos e subcapítulos ativados/descartados.</p> </li> <li data-start="4138" data-end="4204"> <p data-start="4141" data-end="4204">Checklist final é exibido, organizado por seção (R301–R330+).</p> </li> <li data-start="4205" data-end="4264"> <p data-start="4208" data-end="4264">Usuário pode alternar entre modo técnico e modo leigo.</p> </li> <li data-start="4265" data-end="4299"> <p data-start="4268" data-end="4299">Checklist pode ser exportado.</p> </li> </ol> <p data-start="4301" data-end="4324">🔹 <strong data-start="4304" data-end="4322">Escopo Inicial</strong></p> <ul data-start="4325" data-end="4624"> <li data-start="4325" data-end="4370"> <p data-start="4327" data-end="4370">Cobrir o IRC inteiro (não só Capítulo 3).</p> </li> <li data-start="4371" data-end="4403"> <p data-start="4373" data-end="4403">~350–400 condições no banco.</p> </li> <li data-start="4404" data-end="4450"> <p data-start="4406" data-end="4450">Usuário médio responde só 20–40 perguntas.</p> </li> <li data-start="4451" data-end="4624"> <p data-start="4453" data-end="4477">Checklist final cobre:</p> <ul data-start="4480" data-end="4624"> <li data-start="4480" data-end="4493"> <p data-start="4482" data-end="4493">Estrutura</p> </li> <li data-start="4496" data-end="4525"> <p data-start="4498" data-end="4525">Segurança contra incêndio</p> </li> <li data-start="4528" data-end="4546"> <p data-start="4530" data-end="4546">Acessibilidade</p> </li> <li data-start="4549" data-end="4597"> <p data-start="4551" data-end="4597">Instalações (elétrica, hidráulica, mecânica)</p> </li> <li data-start="4600" data-end="4624"> <p data-start="4602" data-end="4624">Energia e ambientais</p> </li> </ul> </li> </ul> <hr data-start="4626" data-end="4629"> <h1 data-start="4631" data-end="4645">🚀 Roadmap</h1> <p data-start="4647" data-end="4668"><strong data-start="4647" data-end="4666">MVP (3–6 meses)</strong></p> <ul data-start="4669" data-end="4820"> <li data-start="4669" data-end="4729"> <p data-start="4671" data-end="4729">Perguntas macro + condições de ativação para todo o IRC.</p> </li> <li data-start="4730" data-end="4788"> <p data-start="4732" data-end="4788">Painel colorido expansível por capítulos/subcapítulos.</p> </li> <li data-start="4789" data-end="4820"> <p data-start="4791" data-end="4820">Checklist final exportável.</p> </li> </ul> <p data-start="4822" data-end="4836"><strong data-start="4822" data-end="4834">Versão 2</strong></p> <ul data-start="4837" data-end="4966"> <li data-start="4837" data-end="4868"> <p data-start="4839" data-end="4868">Editor visual para ajustes.</p> </li> <li data-start="4869" data-end="4940"> <p data-start="4871" data-end="4940">Filtros ambientais regionais (neve, vento, flood, térmitas, radon).</p> </li> <li data-start="4941" data-end="4966"> <p data-start="4943" data-end="4966">Tradução multilíngue.</p> </li> </ul> <p data-start="4968" data-end="4982"><strong data-start="4968" data-end="4980">Versão 3</strong></p> <ul data-start="4983" data-end="5125"> <li data-start="4983" data-end="5048"> <p data-start="4985" data-end="5048">Expansão para outros códigos (IBC, NEC, IECC, normas locais).</p> </li> <li data-start="5049" data-end="5125"> <p data-start="5051" data-end="5125">Marketplace de checklists validadas (engenheiros publicam suas versões).</p> </li> </ul> <hr data-start="5127" data-end="5130"> <p data-start="5132" data-end="5446">👉 <strong data-start="5135" data-end="5152">Resumo final:</strong><br data-start="5152" data-end="5155"> O app não vai auditar medidas, nem usar IA em tempo real.<br data-start="5212" data-end="5215"> Ele vai ser construído em cima de checklists estáticas criadas manualmente, com ajuda da IA apenas no rascunho.<br data-start="5326" data-end="5329"> O foco é filtrar o IRC inteiro em 20–40 perguntas práticas e entregar um checklist final em versão técnica e leiga.</p> <p data-start="5448" data-end="5637">⚠️ <strong data-start="5451" data-end="5471">Nota importante:</strong><br data-start="5471" data-end="5474"> Todas as <strong data-start="5483" data-end="5590">condições de ativação serão fornecidas por mim (engenheiro técnico), já que entendo a parte construtiva</strong>. O programador não precisará conhecer o IRC.</p> <p data-start="5639" data-end="5836">❓ <strong data-start="5641" data-end="5672">Pergunta para o freelancer:</strong> Qual seria a melhor forma de eu fornecer essas condições para você? (Ex.: planilha Excel, JSON, CSV, banco Postgres, ou outro formato que facilite a integração).</p> <hr data-start="5838" data-end="5841"> <p data-start="5843" data-end="6026" data-is-last-node data-is-only-node>Quer que eu adapte essa versão diretamente no <strong data-start="5889" data-end="5921">formato de anúncio do Upwork</strong> (com título e seções próprias de job post) ou você prefere manter o texto mais corrido como um briefing?</p></div></div></div></div><div class="flex min-h-[46px] justify-start"><div class="touch:-me-2 touch:-ms-3.5 -ms-2.5 -me-1 flex flex-wrap items-center gap-y-4 p-1 select-none touch:w-[calc(100%+--spacing(3.5))] -mt-1 w-[calc(100%+--spacing(2.5))] duration-[1.5s] focus-within:transition-none hover:transition-none pointer-events-none data-testid="conversation-turn-3" data-scroll-anchor="false" data-turn="assistant"><h6 class="sr-only">ChatGPT said:</h6><div class="text-base my-auto mx-auto [--thread-content-margin:--spacing(4)] thread-sm:[--thread-content-margin:--spacing(6)] thread-lg:[--thread-content-margin:--spacing(16)] px-(--thread-content-margin)"><div class="[--thread-content-max-width:40rem] thread-sm:[--thread-content-max-width:40rem] thread-lg:[--thread-content-max-width:48rem] mx-auto max-w-(--thread-content-max-width) flex-1 group/turn-messages focus-visible:outline-hidden relative flex w-full min-w-0 flex-col agent-turn" tabindex="-1"><div class="flex max-w-full flex-col grow"><div data-message-author-role="assistant" data-message-id="98c7d1bc-9e0c-4e88-bcc6-554adce7b317" dir="auto" class="min-h-8 text-message relative flex w-full flex-col items-end gap-2 text-start break-words whitespace-normal [.text-message+&amp;]:mt-5" data-message-model-slug="gpt-5"><div class="flex w-full flex-col gap-1 empty:hidden first:pt-[3px]"><div class="markdown prose dark:prose-invert w-full break-words dark markdown-new-styling"><p data-start="0" data-end="98">Great question! 🙂<br data-start="18" data-end="21"> In this context, <strong data-start="38" data-end="45">IRC</strong> stands for the <strong data-start="61" data-end="95">International Residential Code</strong>.</p> <p data-start="100" data-end="125">Here’s a quick breakdown:</p> <ul data-start="127" data-end="978"> <li data-start="127" data-end="235"> <p data-start="129" data-end="235">🏠 <strong data-start="132" data-end="146">What it is</strong>:<br data-start="147" data-end="150"> It’s a model building code published by the <strong data-start="196" data-end="232">International Code Council (ICC)</strong>.</p> </li> <li data-start="237" data-end="566"> <p data-start="239" data-end="376">📖 <strong data-start="242" data-end="260">What it covers</strong>:<br data-start="261" data-end="264"> The IRC sets <strong data-start="279" data-end="302">minimum regulations</strong> for one- and two-family dwellings and townhouses. It includes rules on:</p> <ul data-start="379" data-end="566"> <li data-start="379" data-end="428"> <p data-start="381" data-end="428">Structural design (walls, roofs, foundations)</p> </li> <li data-start="431" data-end="456"> <p data-start="433" data-end="456">Fire safety and exits</p> </li> <li data-start="459" data-end="480"> <p data-start="461" data-end="480">Energy efficiency</p> </li> <li data-start="483" data-end="531"> <p data-start="485" data-end="531">Plumbing, mechanical, and electrical systems</p> </li> <li data-start="534" data-end="566"> <p data-start="536" data-end="566">Accessibility and livability</p> </li> </ul> </li> <li data-start="568" data-end="794"> <p data-start="570" data-end="794">🌎 <strong data-start="573" data-end="592">Where it’s used</strong>:<br data-start="593" data-end="596"> It’s widely adopted across the <strong data-start="629" data-end="646">United States</strong> (and referenced elsewhere) as the standard for residential construction. Local jurisdictions often adopt the IRC either fully or with amendments.</p> </li> <li data-start="796" data-end="978"> <p data-start="798" data-end="978">🔑 <strong data-start="801" data-end="819">Why it matters</strong>:<br data-start="820" data-end="823"> Builders, contractors, engineers, and inspectors rely on the IRC to ensure that homes are safe, durable, and compliant with local building departments.</p> </li> </ul> <p data-start="980" data-end="1066">👉 In short: the <strong data-start="997" data-end="1064">IRC is like the rulebook for building houses safely in the U.S.</strong></p> <p data-start="1068" data-end="1199" data-is-last-node data-is-only-node>Would you like me to also explain how it’s different from the <strong data-start="1130" data-end="1137">IBC</strong> (International Building Code), since they often get confused?</p></div></div></div></div><div class="flex min-h-[46px] justify-start"><div class="touch:-me-2 touch:-ms-3.5 -ms-2.5 -me-1 flex flex-wrap items-center gap-y-4 p-1 select-none touch:w-[calc(100%+--spacing(3.5))] -mt-1 w-[calc(100%+--spacing(2.5))] duration-[1.5s] focus-within:transition-none hover:transition-none pointer-events-none class="text-token-text-primary w-full focus:outline-none scroll-mt-[calc(var(--header-height)+min(200px,max(70px,20svh)))]" tabindex="-1" dir="auto" data-turn-id="045d2fc1-1e19-428d-87df-bb892ec2a26b" data-testid="conversation-turn-5" data-scroll-anchor="false" data-turn="assistant"><h6 class="sr-only">ChatGPT said:</h6><div class="text-base my-auto mx-auto [--thread-content-margin:--spacing(4)] thread-sm:[--thread-content-margin:--spacing(6)] thread-lg:[--thread-content-margin:--spacing(16)] px-(--thread-content-margin)"><div class="[--thread-content-max-width:40rem] thread-sm:[--thread-content-max-width:40rem] thread-lg:[--thread-content-max-width:48rem] mx-auto max-w-(--thread-content-max-width) flex-1 group/turn-messages focus-visible:outline-hidden relative flex w-full min-w-0 flex-col agent-turn" tabindex="-1"><div class="flex max-w-full flex-col grow"><div data-message-author-role="assistant" data-message-id="045d2fc1-1e19-428d-87df-bb892ec2a26b" dir="auto" class="min-h-8 text-message relative flex w-full flex-col items-end gap-2 text-start break-words whitespace-normal [.text-message+&amp;]:mt-5" data-message-model-slug="gpt-5"><div class="flex w-full flex-col gap-1 empty:hidden first:pt-[3px]"><div class="markdown prose dark:prose-invert w-full break-words dark markdown-new-styling"><p data-start="0" data-end="94">Got it — let me unpack this in simpler terms, without assuming civil engineering background.</p> <hr data-start="96" data-end="99"> <h2 data-start="101" data-end="130">🌍 Where the IRC is used</h2> <ul data-start="131" data-end="583"> <li data-start="131" data-end="220"> <p data-start="133" data-end="220">The <strong data-start="137" data-end="177">International Residential Code (IRC)</strong> is <strong data-start="181" data-end="217">mostly used in the United States</strong>.</p> </li> <li data-start="221" data-end="284"> <p data-start="223" data-end="284">It’s published by the <strong data-start="245" data-end="281">International Code Council (ICC)</strong>.</p> </li> <li data-start="285" data-end="411"> <p data-start="287" data-end="411">U.S. states and local governments <strong data-start="321" data-end="375">adopt the IRC (sometimes with small modifications)</strong> to regulate how houses are built.</p> </li> <li data-start="412" data-end="583"> <p data-start="414" data-end="583">Other countries don’t normally adopt the IRC itself — they have their own building codes — but engineers or architects outside the U.S. might look at it for reference.</p> </li> </ul> <p data-start="585" data-end="678">So, think of the IRC as <strong data-start="609" data-end="675">the U.S. “rulebook” for houses and small residential buildings</strong>.</p> <hr data-start="680" data-end="683"> <h2 data-start="685" data-end="720">🛠️ João’s idea in plain words</h2> <p data-start="721" data-end="796">João is trying to <strong data-start="739" data-end="769">make the IRC easier to use</strong>. Right now, the code is:</p> <ul data-start="797" data-end="990"> <li data-start="797" data-end="827"> <p data-start="799" data-end="827">Over <strong data-start="804" data-end="824">1,100 pages long</strong>.</p> </li> <li data-start="828" data-end="883"> <p data-start="830" data-end="883">Full of technical language and legal-style writing.</p> </li> <li data-start="884" data-end="990"> <p data-start="886" data-end="990">Hard for contractors, builders, or even engineers to quickly check what applies to a specific project.</p> </li> </ul> <h3 data-start="992" data-end="1012">🔹 The Problem</h3> <p data-start="1013" data-end="1093">Imagine you’re building or renovating a house.<br data-start="1059" data-end="1062"> You need to know things like:</p> <ul data-start="1094" data-end="1286"> <li data-start="1094" data-end="1155"> <p data-start="1096" data-end="1155">What fire exits are required if the house has a basement.</p> </li> <li data-start="1156" data-end="1200"> <p data-start="1158" data-end="1200">What the minimum ceiling height must be.</p> </li> <li data-start="1201" data-end="1232"> <p data-start="1203" data-end="1232">How wide stairs need to be.</p> </li> <li data-start="1233" data-end="1286"> <p data-start="1235" data-end="1286">If a swimming pool requires a protective barrier.</p> </li> </ul> <p data-start="1288" data-end="1377">The IRC has the answers — but <strong data-start="1318" data-end="1374">finding them means digging through hundreds of pages</strong>.</p> <h3 data-start="1379" data-end="1418">🔹 The Solution (João’s app idea)</h3> <p data-start="1419" data-end="1469">Instead of reading the whole book, the app will:</p> <ol data-start="1470" data-end="2456"> <li data-start="1470" data-end="1649"> <p data-start="1473" data-end="1504"><strong data-start="1473" data-end="1501">Ask you simple questions</strong>:</p> <ul data-start="1508" data-end="1649"> <li data-start="1508" data-end="1543"> <p data-start="1510" data-end="1543">Does the house have a basement?</p> </li> <li data-start="1547" data-end="1576"> <p data-start="1549" data-end="1576">Is there a swimming pool?</p> </li> <li data-start="1580" data-end="1610"> <p data-start="1582" data-end="1610">How many floors are there?</p> </li> <li data-start="1614" data-end="1649"> <p data-start="1616" data-end="1649">Is it in a snowy or windy area?</p> </li> </ul> </li> <li data-start="1651" data-end="1888"> <p data-start="1654" data-end="1690"><strong data-start="1654" data-end="1687">Filter the code automatically</strong>:</p> <ul data-start="1694" data-end="1888"> <li data-start="1694" data-end="1816"> <p data-start="1696" data-end="1816">If you say “yes, it has a basement with bedrooms,” the app will highlight the rule about <strong data-start="1785" data-end="1813">emergency basement exits</strong>.</p> </li> <li data-start="1820" data-end="1888"> <p data-start="1822" data-end="1888">If you say “no swimming pool,” the app hides those requirements.</p> </li> </ul> </li> <li data-start="1890" data-end="2201"> <p data-start="1893" data-end="1916"><strong data-start="1893" data-end="1913">Show a checklist</strong>:</p> <ul data-start="1920" data-end="2201"> <li data-start="1920" data-end="1993"> <p data-start="1922" data-end="1993">A tailored list of all the rules <strong data-start="1955" data-end="1990">that apply only to your project</strong>.</p> </li> <li data-start="1997" data-end="2201"> <p data-start="1999" data-end="2029">Rules are shown in two ways:</p> <ul data-start="2035" data-end="2201"> <li data-start="2035" data-end="2109"> <p data-start="2037" data-end="2109"><strong data-start="2037" data-end="2058">Technical version</strong> (exact legal/engineering language from the IRC).</p> </li> <li data-start="2115" data-end="2201"> <p data-start="2117" data-end="2201"><strong data-start="2117" data-end="2135">Simple version</strong> (translated into plain words, so non-engineers can understand).</p> </li> </ul> </li> </ul> </li> <li data-start="2203" data-end="2456"> <p data-start="2206" data-end="2225"><strong data-start="2206" data-end="2222">Visual panel</strong>:</p> <ul data-start="2229" data-end="2456"> <li data-start="2229" data-end="2315"> <p data-start="2231" data-end="2315">The whole IRC is broken down into chapters (e.g., fire safety, energy, structure).</p> </li> <li data-start="2319" data-end="2456"> <p data-start="2321" data-end="2346">The app colors them as:</p> <ul data-start="2352" data-end="2456"> <li data-start="2352" data-end="2383"> <p data-start="2354" data-end="2383"><strong data-start="2354" data-end="2363">Green</strong> = applies to you.</p> </li> <li data-start="2389" data-end="2417"> <p data-start="2391" data-end="2417"><strong data-start="2391" data-end="2398">Red</strong> = doesn’t apply.</p> </li> <li data-start="2423" data-end="2456"> <p data-start="2425" data-end="2456"><strong data-start="2425" data-end="2433">Grey</strong> = not evaluated yet.</p> </li> </ul> </li> </ul> </li> </ol> <hr data-start="2458" data-end="2461"> <h2 data-start="2463" data-end="2486">🎯 Why it’s useful</h2> <ul data-start="2487" data-end="2814"> <li data-start="2487" data-end="2563"> <p data-start="2489" data-end="2563">Saves <strong data-start="2495" data-end="2503">time</strong>: you don’t read 1,100 pages, just answer 20–40 questions.</p> </li> <li data-start="2564" data-end="2633"> <p data-start="2566" data-end="2633">Reduces <strong data-start="2574" data-end="2586">mistakes</strong>: contractors won’t miss hidden requirements.</p> </li> <li data-start="2634" data-end="2740"> <p data-start="2636" data-end="2740">Makes compliance <strong data-start="2653" data-end="2667">accessible</strong>: both professionals and homeowners can see the rules in plain English.</p> </li> <li data-start="2741" data-end="2814"> <p data-start="2743" data-end="2814">Can expand later to other codes (commercial buildings, energy, etc.).</p> </li> </ul> <hr data-start="2816" data-end="2819"> <p data-start="2821" data-end="3055">✅ <strong data-start="2823" data-end="2835">In short</strong>: The IRC is the U.S. rulebook for houses. João’s idea is an app that turns this giant, hard-to-use book into a <strong data-start="2947" data-end="2990">dynamic questionnaire + smart checklist</strong>, so anyone can quickly see which rules apply to their project.</p> <p data-start="3057" data-end="3206" data-is-last-node data-is-only-node>Would you like me to sketch out a <strong data-start="3091" data-end="3120">step-by-step user journey</strong> (like what happens when someone actually uses the app) to make it even more concrete?</p></div></div></div></div><div class="flex min-h-[46px] justify-start"><div class="touch:-me-2 touch:-ms-3.5 -ms-2.5 -me-1 flex flex-wrap items-center gap-y-4 p-1 select-none touch:w-[calc(100%+--spacing(3.5))] -mt-1 w-[calc(100%+--spacing(2.5))] duration-[1.5s] focus-within:transition-none class="text-token-text-primary w-full focus:outline-none scroll-mt-[calc(var(--header-height)+min(200px,max(70px,20svh)))]" tabindex="-1" dir="auto" data-turn-id="ec4ea2ef-ff7d-400a-99b4-13e393112267" data-testid="conversation-turn-7" data-scroll-anchor="false" data-turn="assistant"><h6 class="sr-only">ChatGPT said:</h6><div class="text-base my-auto mx-auto [--thread-content-margin:--spacing(4)] thread-sm:[--thread-content-margin:--spacing(6)] thread-lg:[--thread-content-margin:--spacing(16)] px-(--thread-content-margin)"><div class="[--thread-content-max-width:40rem] thread-sm:[--thread-content-max-width:40rem] thread-lg:[--thread-content-max-width:48rem] mx-auto max-w-(--thread-content-max-width) flex-1 group/turn-messages focus-visible:outline-hidden relative flex w-full min-w-0 flex-col agent-turn" tabindex="-1"><div class="flex max-w-full flex-col grow"><div data-message-author-role="assistant" data-message-id="ec4ea2ef-ff7d-400a-99b4-13e393112267" dir="auto" class="min-h-8 text-message relative flex w-full flex-col items-end gap-2 text-start break-words whitespace-normal [.text-message+&amp;]:mt-5" data-message-model-slug="gpt-5"><div class="flex w-full flex-col gap-1 empty:hidden first:pt-[3px]"><div class="markdown prose dark:prose-invert w-full break-words dark markdown-new-styling"><p data-start="0" data-end="106">Excellent question — this is really about <strong data-start="42" data-end="82">balancing reliability vs. innovation</strong>. Let’s break it down.</p> <hr data-start="108" data-end="111"> <h2 data-start="113" data-end="150">🔹 Option 1: Manual Reading + UI</h2> <p data-start="151" data-end="357">You and your team <strong data-start="169" data-end="194">manually read the IRC</strong>, extract the rules, and encode them into a structured database (e.g., JSON or Postgres). The UI then just presents those rules with conditional logic (if/else).</p> <h3 data-start="359" data-end="371">✅ Pros</h3> <ul data-start="372" data-end="820"> <li data-start="372" data-end="464"> <p data-start="374" data-end="464"><strong data-start="374" data-end="391">100% reliable</strong>: no risk of AI misinterpreting a rule — engineers control the content.</p> </li> <li data-start="465" data-end="622"> <p data-start="467" data-end="622"><strong data-start="467" data-end="484">Legally safer</strong>: if this product is ever used in a professional context, being able to say “this comes directly from the official IRC text” is crucial.</p> </li> <li data-start="623" data-end="697"> <p data-start="625" data-end="697"><strong data-start="625" data-end="647">Consistent results</strong>: every user sees the same validated checklists.</p> </li> <li data-start="698" data-end="820"> <p data-start="700" data-end="820"><strong data-start="700" data-end="720">Regulatory trust</strong>: inspectors, contractors, and engineers will prefer something that looks official and verifiable.</p> </li> </ul> <h3 data-start="822" data-end="834">❌ Cons</h3> <ul data-start="835" data-end="1136"> <li data-start="835" data-end="921"> <p data-start="837" data-end="921"><strong data-start="837" data-end="863">Time-consuming upfront</strong>: reading and structuring 1,100+ pages is a lot of work.</p> </li> <li data-start="922" data-end="1045"> <p data-start="924" data-end="1045"><strong data-start="924" data-end="943">Harder to scale</strong>: if you want to add other codes (IBC, ISO, local variations), you must repeat the process manually.</p> </li> <li data-start="1046" data-end="1136"> <p data-start="1048" data-end="1136"><strong data-start="1048" data-end="1058">Static</strong>: when a new version of the IRC is released, you must manually update again.</p> </li> </ul> <hr data-start="1138" data-end="1141"> <h2 data-start="1143" data-end="1183">🔹 Option 2: AI-Assisted Extraction</h2> <p data-start="1184" data-end="1235">Use AI (LLMs + NLP) to read the IRC and generate:</p> <ul data-start="1236" data-end="1371"> <li data-start="1236" data-end="1266"> <p data-start="1238" data-end="1266">Summaries of each section.</p> </li> <li data-start="1267" data-end="1336"> <p data-start="1269" data-end="1336">Conditional rules (“if there’s a basement bedroom → apply R310”).</p> </li> <li data-start="1337" data-end="1371"> <p data-start="1339" data-end="1371">Checklists in simple language.</p> </li> </ul> <p data-start="1373" data-end="1435">Engineers then <strong data-start="1388" data-end="1411">review and validate</strong> what the AI produced.</p> <h3 data-start="1437" data-end="1449">✅ Pros</h3> <ul data-start="1450" data-end="1668"> <li data-start="1450" data-end="1521"> <p data-start="1452" data-end="1521"><strong data-start="1452" data-end="1467">Much faster</strong>: AI can parse the 1,100 pages in hours, not months.</p> </li> <li data-start="1522" data-end="1590"> <p data-start="1524" data-end="1590"><strong data-start="1524" data-end="1536">Scalable</strong>: adding other codes (IBC, ISO, NEC) becomes easier.</p> </li> <li data-start="1591" data-end="1668"> <p data-start="1593" data-end="1668"><strong data-start="1593" data-end="1618">Keeps up with updates</strong>: when a new IRC is released, rerun the AI pass.</p> </li> </ul> <h3 data-start="1670" data-end="1682">❌ Cons</h3> <ul data-start="1683" data-end="1920"> <li data-start="1683" data-end="1760"> <p data-start="1685" data-end="1760"><strong data-start="1685" data-end="1703">Risk of errors</strong>: AI might miss nuances, exceptions, or legal language.</p> </li> <li data-start="1761" data-end="1837"> <p data-start="1763" data-end="1837"><strong data-start="1763" data-end="1779">Trust issues</strong>: professionals may not trust “AI-generated compliance.”</p> </li> <li data-start="1838" data-end="1920"> <p data-start="1840" data-end="1920"><strong data-start="1840" data-end="1865">Extra validation step</strong>: you’ll still need an engineer to review everything.</p> </li> </ul> <hr data-start="1922" data-end="1925"> <h2 data-start="1927" data-end="1964">🔹 Hybrid (Recommended Strategy)</h2> <p data-start="1965" data-end="2019">The strongest path is usually a <strong data-start="1997" data-end="2016">hybrid approach</strong>:</p> <ol data-start="2021" data-end="2712"> <li data-start="2021" data-end="2201"> <p data-start="2024" data-end="2054"><strong data-start="2024" data-end="2052">Use AI in the background</strong></p> <ul data-start="2058" data-end="2201"> <li data-start="2058" data-end="2129"> <p data-start="2060" data-end="2129">Extract and summarize the IRC into draft checklists and conditions.</p> </li> <li data-start="2133" data-end="2201"> <p data-start="2135" data-end="2201">Identify cross-references (“see section R310.1 for exceptions”).</p> </li> </ul> </li> <li data-start="2203" data-end="2371"> <p data-start="2206" data-end="2241"><strong data-start="2206" data-end="2239">Engineer validates everything</strong></p> <ul data-start="2245" data-end="2371"> <li data-start="2245" data-end="2311"> <p data-start="2247" data-end="2311">A civil engineer (like João) reviews, corrects, and signs off.</p> </li> <li data-start="2315" data-end="2371"> <p data-start="2317" data-end="2371">Final database is <strong data-start="2335" data-end="2368">human-validated, legally safe</strong>.</p> </li> </ul> </li> <li data-start="2373" data-end="2554"> <p data-start="2376" data-end="2408"><strong data-start="2376" data-end="2406">Ship a stable, reliable UI</strong></p> <ul data-start="2412" data-end="2554"> <li data-start="2412" data-end="2483"> <p data-start="2414" data-end="2483">The app only uses the <strong data-start="2436" data-end="2458">validated database</strong>, never raw AI answers.</p> </li> <li data-start="2487" data-end="2554"> <p data-start="2489" data-end="2554">Users get <strong data-start="2499" data-end="2524">deterministic results</strong>, not probabilistic guesses.</p> </li> </ul> </li> <li data-start="2556" data-end="2712"> <p data-start="2559" data-end="2598"><strong data-start="2559" data-end="2596">Keep AI for internal productivity</strong></p> <ul data-start="2602" data-end="2712"> <li data-start="2602" data-end="2657"> <p data-start="2604" data-end="2657">AI is your <strong data-start="2615" data-end="2630">accelerator</strong>, not the product itself.</p> </li> <li data-start="2661" data-end="2712"> <p data-start="2663" data-end="2712">Engineers decide what makes it into production.</p> </li> </ul> </li> </ol> <hr data-start="2714" data-end="2717"> <h2 data-start="2719" data-end="2741">🎯 Recommendation</h2> <ul data-start="2742" data-end="3093"> <li data-start="2742" data-end="2928"> <p data-start="2744" data-end="2928">For an <strong data-start="2751" data-end="2758">MVP</strong> → Start <strong data-start="2767" data-end="2783">manual-first</strong>. Create the database yourself (even if smaller, e.g., only covering the most common 50 rules). That way you prove reliability and build trust.</p> </li> <li data-start="2929" data-end="3008"> <p data-start="2931" data-end="3008">Use <strong data-start="2935" data-end="2952">AI internally</strong> to speed up reading, but never as the “final source.”</p> </li> <li data-start="3009" data-end="3093"> <p data-start="3011" data-end="3093">As you grow → gradually scale with AI support, but always with human validation.</p> </li> </ul> <hr data-start="3095" data-end="3098"> <p data-start="3100" data-end="3116">✅ <strong data-start="3102" data-end="3114">Summary:</strong></p> <ul data-start="3117" data-end="3259"> <li data-start="3117" data-end="3164"> <p data-start="3119" data-end="3164">Manual-first = maximum trust, slow scaling.</p> </li> <li data-start="3165" data-end="3196"> <p data-start="3167" data-end="3196">AI-first = fast, but risky.</p> </li> <li data-start="3197" data-end="3259"> <p data-start="3199" data-end="3259"><strong data-start="3199" data-end="3231">Hybrid = best of both worlds</strong> → reliable, but scalable.</p> </li> </ul> <hr data-start="3261" data-end="3264"> <p data-start="3266" data-end="3415" data-is-last-node data-is-only-node>Would you like me to outline a <strong data-start="3297" data-end="3321">step-by-step roadmap</strong> for this hybrid approach (e.g., how to start manual, introduce AI, and scale to other codes)?</p></div></div></div></div><div class="flex min-h-[46px] justify-start"><div class="touch:-me-2 touch:-ms-3.5 -ms-2.5 -me-1 flex flex-wrap items-center gap-y-4 p-1 select-none touch:w-[calc(100%+--spacing(3.5))] -mt-1 w-[calc(100%+--spacing(2.5))] duration-[1.5s] focus-within:transition-none hover:transition-none pointer-events-none class="text-token-text-primary w-full focus:outline-none scroll-mt-[calc(var(--header-height)+min(200px,max(70px,20svh)))]" tabindex="-1" dir="auto" data-turn-id="3c36dbd9-24c0-4609-994e-1fc467fc0ad3" data-testid="conversation-turn-9" data-scroll-anchor="false" data-turn="assistant"><h6 class="sr-only">ChatGPT said:</h6><div class="text-base my-auto mx-auto [--thread-content-margin:--spacing(4)] flex w-full min-w-0 flex-col agent-turn" tabindex="-1"><div class="flex max-w-full flex-col grow"><div data-message-author-role="assistant" data-message-id="634b078b-8969-4e46-ac75-5ad271cde900" dir="auto" class="min-h-8 text-message relative flex w-full flex-col items-end gap-2 text-start break-words whitespace-normal [.text-message+&amp;]:mt-5" data-message-model-slug="gpt-5"><div class="flex w-full flex-col gap-1 empty:hidden first:pt-[3px]"><div class="markdown prose dark:prose-invert w-full break-words dark markdown-new-styling"><p data-start="0" data-end="164">Yes — there <em data-start="12" data-end="17">are</em> products out there with similar goals, and yes, there <em data-start="72" data-end="77">are</em> ways to get some kind of certification or credibility. I’ll split this into two parts:</p> <hr data-start="166" data-end="169"> <h2 data-start="171" data-end="208">🧐 Existing Products / Competitors</h2> <p data-start="210" data-end="297">Here are a few tools and software that already do parts of what João’s idea aims to do:</p> <div class="_tableContainer_1rjym_1"><div tabindex="-1" class="group w-fit _tableWrapper_1rjym_13 flex flex-col-reverse"><table data-start="299" data-end="1686" class="w-fit min-w-(--thread-content-width)"><thead data-start="299" data-end="364"><tr data-start="299" data-end="364"><th data-start="299" data-end="316" data-col-size="md">Tool / Product</th><th data-start="316" data-end="336" data-col-size="xl">What they do well</th><th data-start="336" data-end="364" data-col-size="lg">How close to João’s idea</th></tr></thead><tbody data-start="379" data-end="1686"><tr data-start="379" data-end="760"><td data-start="379" data-end="398" data-col-size="md"><strong data-start="381" data-end="397">Plan Analyst</strong></td><td data-start="398" data-end="614" data-col-size="xl">Software for building officials, architects, etc., to run “plan checks” (reviewing building plans) and generate code-correctness / compliance reports based on the IRC and IBC. <span class data-state="closed"><span class="ms-1 inline-flex max-w-full items-center relative top-[-0.094rem] animate-[show_150ms_ease-in]" data-testid="webpage-citation-pill"><a href="https://www.plananalyst.com/?utm_source=chatgpt.com" target="_blank" rel="noopener" alt="https://www.plananalyst.com/?utm_source=chatgpt.com" class="flex h-4.5 overflow-hidden rounded-xl px-2 text-[9px] font-medium text-token-text-secondary! bg-[#F4F4F4]! dark:bg-[#303030]! transition-colors duration-150 ease-in-out"><span class="relative start-0 bottom-0 flex h-full w-full items-center"><span class="flex h-4 w-full items-center justify-between overflow-hidden" style="opacity:1;transform:none;"><span class="max-w-[15ch] grow truncate overflow-hidden text-center">Plan Analyst</span></span></span></a></span></span></td><td data-start="614" data-end="760" data-col-size="lg">Very close in the sense of automatic checking of building rules, but might not have the dynamic questionnaire + visual panel style João wants.</td></tr><tr data-start="761" data-end="1109"><td data-start="761" data-end="774" data-col-size="md"><strong data-start="763" data-end="773">FTQ360</strong></td><td data-start="774" data-end="956" data-col-size="xl">For factory-built / modular homes: digital checklists, inspections, making sure each unit checks all relevant codes (IRC, IBC, energy, etc.). <span class data-state="closed"><span class="ms-1 inline-flex max-w-full items-center relative top-[-0.094rem] animate-[show_150ms_ease-in]" data-testid="webpage-citation-pill"><a href="https://info.ftq360.com/factory-built-housing-software?utm_source=chatgpt.com" target="_blank" rel="noopener" alt="https://info.ftq360.com/factory-built-housing-software?utm_source=chatgpt.com" class="flex h-4.5 overflow-hidden rounded-xl px-2 text-[9px] font-medium text-token-text-secondary! bg-[#F4F4F4]! dark:bg-[#303030]! transition-colors duration-150 ease-in-out"><span class="relative start-0 bottom-0 flex h-full w-full items-center"><span class="flex h-4 w-full items-center justify-between overflow-hidden" style="opacity:1;transform:none;"><span class="max-w-[15ch] grow truncate overflow-hidden text-center">FTQ360 Info</span></span></span></a></span></span></td><td data-start="956" data-end="1109" data-col-size="lg">This is also close; they embed code compliance into workflows, but more for production/inspection rather than for planning via interactive checklist.</td></tr><tr data-start="1110" data-end="1448"><td data-start="1110" data-end="1136" data-col-size="md"><strong data-start="1112" data-end="1135">REScheck / COMcheck</strong></td><td data-start="1136" data-end="1315" data-col-size="xl">U.S. government tools for checking energy-code compliance of building envelopes (walls, windows) for residential and commercial buildings. <span class data-state="closed"><span class="ms-1 inline-flex max-w-full items-center relative top-[-0.094rem] animate-[show_150ms_ease-in]" data-testid="webpage-citation-pill"><a href="https://www.energycodes.gov/rescheck?utm_source=chatgpt.com" target="_blank" rel="noopener" alt="https://www.energycodes.gov/rescheck?utm_source=chatgpt.com" class="flex h-4.5 overflow-hidden rounded-xl px-2 text-[9px] font-medium text-token-text-secondary! bg-[#F4F4F4]! dark:bg-[#303030]! transition-colors duration-150 ease-in-out"><span class="relative start-0 bottom-0 flex h-full w-full items-center"><span class="flex h-4 w-full items-center justify-between" style="opacity:1;transform:none;"><span class="max-w-[15ch] grow truncate overflow-hidden text-center">Energy Codes</span><span class="-me-1 flex h-full items-center rounded-full px-1 text-[#8F8F8F]">+1</span></span></span></a></span></span></td><td data-start="1315" data-end="1448" data-col-size="lg">They only cover energy / thermal / envelope parts, not <em data-start="1372" data-end="1377">all</em> of IRC. But they show that government-backed compliance tools exist.</td></tr><tr data-start="1449" data-end="1686"><td data-start="1449" data-end="1500" data-col-size="md"><strong data-start="1451" data-end="1499">Building Compliance Systems (Australia etc.)</strong></td><td data-start="1500" data-end="1620" data-col-size="xl">Offer software for certification / approval / permits, managing inspectors etc. <span class data-state="closed"><span class="ms-1 inline-flex max-w-full items-center relative top-[-0.094rem] animate-[show_150ms_ease-in]" data-testid="webpage-citation-pill"><a href="https://www.buildingcompliance.systems/?utm_source=chatgpt.com" target="_blank" rel="noopener" alt="https://www.buildingcompliance.systems/?utm_source=chatgpt.com" class="flex h-4.5 overflow-hidden rounded-xl px-2 text-[9px] font-medium text-token-text-secondary! bg-[#F4F4F4]! dark:bg-[#303030]! transition-colors duration-150 ease-in-out"><span class="relative start-0 bottom-0 text-center">Building Compliance Systems</span><span class="-me-1 flex h-full items-center rounded-full px-1 text-[#8F8F8F]">+1</span></span></span></a></span></span></td><td data-start="1620" data-end="1686" data-col-size="lg">But these are more oriented to workflows of local governments.</td></tr></tbody></table><div class="sticky h-0 select-none end-(--thread-content-margin) 1.696v2.833z"/></svg></button></span></div></div></div></div> <p data-start="1688" data-end="1854">So yes — nobody seems to do <em data-start="1716" data-end="1725">exactly</em> João’s full idea (questionnaire, visual tree, full IRC + leiga vs técnica, etc.), which is good — there's room to differentiate.</p> <hr data-start="1856" data-end="1859"> <h2 data-start="1861" data-end="1901">✅ Certification, Credibility, &amp; Trust</h2> <p data-start="1903" data-end="2130">If you're building something like this, you’ll want users (engineers, inspectors, possibly clients, regulators) to trust that the app's checklists are correct. Here are routes to build that trust and maybe formal certification:</p> <div class="_tableContainer_1rjym_1"><div tabindex="-1" class="group w-fit _tableWrapper_1rjym_13 flex flex-col-reverse"><table data-start="2132" data-end="3791" class="w-fit min-w-(--thread-content-width)"><thead data-start="2132" data-end="2172"><tr data-start="2132" data-end="2172"><th data-start="2132" data-end="2141" data-col-size="md">Method</th><th data-start="2141" data-end="2157" data-col-size="xl">What it means</th><th data-start="2157" data-end="2172" data-col-size="lg">Pros / Cons</th></tr></thead><tbody data-start="2187" data-end="3791"><tr data-start="2187" data-end="2525"><td data-start="2187" data-end="2250" data-col-size="md"><strong data-start="2189" data-end="2249">Third-party review / validation by certified engineer(s)</strong></td><td data-start="2250" data-end="2423" data-col-size="xl">Have recognized experts in the relevant building code (IRC) audit your checklist logic, rules, decisions. Possibly publish a “validated by X engineer / firm” endorsement.</td><td data-start="2423" data-end="2525" data-col-size="lg">Pro: adds professional credibility. Con: cost, time, requires ongoing maintenance if code updates.</td></tr><tr data-start="2526" data-end="2831"><td data-start="2526" data-end="2565" data-col-size="md"><strong data-start="2528" data-end="2564">Government or code body approval</strong></td><td data-start="2565" data-end="2722" data-col-size="xl">If possible, have a building code council / municipal authority review or certify your tool for use, or list it as “accepted method” in that jurisdiction.</td><td data-start="2722" data-end="2831" data-col-size="lg">Huge trust boost, may be bureaucratic and slow; might require compliance with legal/regulatory standards.</td></tr><tr data-start="2832" data-end="3147"><td data-start="2832" data-end="2882" data-col-size="md"><strong data-start="2834" data-end="2881">Standards / software certification programs</strong></td><td data-start="2882" data-end="3057" data-col-size="xl">Participate in standards bodies or software certification lenses. For example, “software certification for compliance tools” or “open standards compliance” (if applicable).</td><td data-start="3057" data-end="3147" data-col-size="lg">Adds credibility, makes selling / licensing easier. Takes effort to satisfy standards.</td></tr><tr data-start="3148" data-end="3428"><td data-start="3148" data-end="3183" data-col-size="md"><strong data-start="3150" data-end="3182">User auditing + transparency</strong></td><td data-start="3183" data-end="3354" data-col-size="xl">Show clearly how rules were derived, versions of the code used, allow export, logs, versioning etc. Possibly open-some part of the rules so users can check correctness.</td><td data-start="3354" data-end="3428" data-col-size="lg">Helps build trust; not formal certification but important in practice.</td></tr><tr data-start="3429" data-end="3791"><td data-start="3429" data-end="3472" data-col-size="md"><strong data-start="3431" data-end="3471">Accreditation / continuing education</strong></td><td data-start="3472" data-end="3688" data-col-size="xl">Get certifications for the team (e.g. ICC credentialing, or other known certifications in building codes) so customers know you have expert staff who understand the code well. <span class data-state="closed"><span class="ms-1 inline-flex max-w-full items-center relative top-[-0.094rem] animate-[show_150ms_ease-in]" data-testid="webpage-citation-pill"><a href="https://www.iccsafe.org/professional-development/certifications-and-testing/credentialing/?utm_source=chatgpt.com" target="_blank" rel="noopener" alt="https://www.iccsafe.org/professional-development/certifications-and-testing/credentialing/?utm_source=chatgpt.com" class="flex h-4.5 overflow-hidden rounded-xl px-2 text-[9px] font-medium text-token-text-secondary! bg-[#F4F4F4]! dark:bg-[#303030]! transition-colors duration-150 ease-in-out"><span class="relative start-0 bottom-0 flex h-full w-full items-center"><span class="flex h-4 w-full items-center justify-between overflow-hidden" style="opacity:1;transform:none;"><span class="max-w-[15ch] grow truncate overflow-hidden text-center">ICC</span></span></span></a></span></span></td><td data-start="3688" data-end="3791" data-col-size="lg">Good for credibility; doesn’t certify the product per se but gives confidence in your capabilities.</td></tr></tbody></table><div class="sticky h-0 select-none end-(--thread-content-margin) self-end"><div class="absolute end-0 flex items-end" style="height:33px;"><span class data-state="closed"><button aria-label="Copy table" class="hover:bg-token-bg-tertiary text-token-text-secondary my-1 rounded-sm p-1 transition-opacity 1.696v2.833z"/></svg></button></span></div></div></div></div> <hr data-start="3793" data-end="3796"> <h2 data-start="3798" data-end="3864">⚙️ What Might Be the Best Certification Path for João’s Product</h2> <p data-start="3866" data-end="3942">Given that you want a <em data-start="3888" data-end="3917">strong and reliable product</em>, here’s a possible path:</p> <ol data-start="3944" data-end="5375"> <li data-start="3944" data-end="4090"> <p data-start="3947" data-end="4090"><strong data-start="3947" data-end="3987">Build a minimum viable product (MVP)</strong> with manual-validated rules (as discussed earlier), covering the most common IRC sections / use cases.</p> </li> <li data-start="4092" data-end="4254"> <p data-start="4095" data-end="4254"><strong data-start="4095" data-end="4134">Recruit an expert (or expert panel)</strong>: one or more civil/structural engineers with IRC experience to audit all the rule logic, checklists, conditional flows.</p> </li> <li data-start="4256" data-end="4450"> <p data-start="4259" data-end="4285"><strong data-start="4259" data-end="4282">Document everything</strong>:</p> <ul data-start="4289" data-end="4450"> <li data-start="4289" data-end="4323"> <p data-start="4291" data-end="4323">which version of IRC you used,</p> </li> <li data-start="4327" data-end="4368"> <p data-start="4329" data-end="4368">how each rule is mapped to questions,</p> </li> <li data-start="4372" data-end="4404"> <p data-start="4374" data-end="4404">what exceptions are handled,</p> </li> <li data-start="4408" data-end="4450"> <p data-start="4410" data-end="4450">what is deferred / what is out of scope.</p> </li> </ul> </li> <li data-start="4452" data-end="4626"> <p data-start="4455" data-end="4626"><strong data-start="4455" data-end="4489">Legal / disclaimer protections</strong>: clarify that the tool provides guidance / checklist, but users are responsible to verify, get local approvals. Might need legal review.</p> </li> <li data-start="4628" data-end="4829"> <p data-start="4631" data-end="4829"><strong data-start="4631" data-end="4665">Seek endorsement / partnership</strong>: possibly with a recognized engineering society or building code council. Even just a “partnered with ____” or “approved for use by ____ jurisdiction” if possible.</p> </li> <li data-start="4831" data-end="5060"> <p data-start="4834" data-end="5060"><strong data-start="4834" data-end="4877">Use standard software quality practices</strong>: test cases, edge cases, version control; allow tracing of changes when the code updates. Possibly certify your software under some standard (if available) about safety, reliability.</p> </li> <li data-start="5062" data-end="5231"> <p data-start="5065" data-end="5231"><strong data-start="5065" data-end="5108">Customer/market feedback / case studies</strong>: use real-projects to show that when people use your app they avoid mistakes / speed up permitting etc. Testimonials help.</p> </li> <li data-start="5233" data-end="5375"> <p data-start="5236" data-end="5375"><strong data-start="5236" data-end="5275">Continuous maintenance &amp; monitoring</strong>: ensure that when the IRC is updated (new edition) you update the rules, re-validate, inform users.</p> </li> </ol> <hr data-start="5377" data-end="5380"> <p data-start="5382" data-end="5662">If you want, I can try to find specific existing certification programs or legal approval paths in <em data-start="5481" data-end="5489">Brazil</em>, so you know what’s required locally (since IRC is U.S. code, maybe you’d adapt for Brazilian norms)? That way you have a roadmap specific to your market. Do you want that?</p> </div></div></div></div><div class="flex min-h-[46px] justify-start"><div class="touch:-me-2 touch:-ms-3.5 -ms-2.5 -me-1 flex flex-wrap items-center gap-y-4 p-1 select-none class="text-token-text-primary w-full focus:outline-none scroll-mt-[calc(var(--header-height)+min(200px,max(70px,20svh)))]" tabindex="-1" dir="auto" data-turn-id="bd4811e4-b5bc-42dd-b970-d097f656fa49" data-testid="conversation-turn-11" data-scroll-anchor="false" data-turn="assistant"><h6 class="sr-only">ChatGPT said:</h6><div class="text-base my-auto mx-auto [--thread-content-margin:--spacing(4)] thread-sm:[--thread-content-margin:--spacing(6)] thread-lg:[--thread-content-margin:--spacing(16)] px-(--thread-content-margin)"><div class="[--thread-content-max-width:40rem] thread-sm:[--thread-content-max-width:40rem] thread-lg:[--thread-content-max-width:48rem] mx-auto max-w-(--thread-content-max-width) flex-1 group/turn-messages focus-visible:outline-hidden relative flex w-full min-w-0 flex-col agent-turn" tabindex="-1"><div class="flex max-w-full flex-col grow"><div data-message-author-role="assistant" data-message-id="bd4811e4-b5bc-42dd-b970-d097f656fa49" dir="auto" class="min-h-8 text-message relative flex w-full flex-col items-end gap-2 text-start break-words whitespace-normal [.text-message+&amp;]:mt-5" data-message-model-slug="gpt-5"><div class="flex w-full flex-col gap-1 empty:hidden first:pt-[3px]"><div class="markdown prose dark:prose-invert w-full break-words dark markdown-new-styling"><p data-start="0" data-end="95">That’s a <em data-start="9" data-end="17">really</em> important question. Here’s how it works with the <strong data-start="67" data-end="94">IRC and other ICC codes</strong>:</p> <hr data-start="97" data-end="100"> <h2 data-start="102" data-end="127">📖 Who owns the IRC?</h2> <ul data-start="128" data-end="388"> <li data-start="128" data-end="212"> <p data-start="130" data-end="212">The <strong data-start="134" data-end="170">International Code Council (ICC)</strong> publishes the IRC (and IBC, IMC, etc.).</p> </li> <li data-start="213" data-end="317"> <p data-start="215" data-end="317">The IRC is considered an <strong data-start="240" data-end="255">adopted law</strong> in many U.S. jurisdictions once local governments adopt it.</p> </li> <li data-start="318" data-end="388"> <p data-start="320" data-end="388">But the <strong data-start="328" data-end="360">copyright of the text itself</strong> still belongs to the ICC.</p> </li> </ul> <hr data-start="390" data-end="393"> <h2 data-start="395" data-end="432">💰 Do you need to pay royalties?</h2> <p data-start="433" data-end="466">It depends on <strong data-start="447" data-end="465">how you use it</strong>:</p> <ol data-start="468" data-end="1449"> <li data-start="468" data-end="894"> <p data-start="471" data-end="526"><strong data-start="471" data-end="524">If you just use the rules as ideas / requirements</strong></p> <ul data-start="530" data-end="894"> <li data-start="530" data-end="630"> <p data-start="532" data-end="630">Laws and regulations themselves are <strong data-start="568" data-end="589">not copyrightable</strong> in the U.S. (Supreme Court precedent).</p> </li> <li data-start="634" data-end="784"> <p data-start="636" data-end="784">That means: you can <em data-start="656" data-end="663">apply</em> the rules, build logic, ask “does the project have a basement?” → then point out “if yes, must have an egress window.”</p> </li> <li data-start="788" data-end="894"> <p data-start="790" data-end="894">✅ You don’t need to pay royalties for creating a checklist or software that operationalizes the rules.</p> </li> </ul> </li> <li data-start="896" data-end="1212"> <p data-start="899" data-end="949"><strong data-start="899" data-end="947">If you reproduce large parts of the IRC text</strong></p> <ul data-start="953" data-end="1212"> <li data-start="953" data-end="1039"> <p data-start="955" data-end="1039">Copy-pasting big sections of the IRC into your app <strong data-start="1006" data-end="1036">may infringe ICC copyright</strong>.</p> </li> <li data-start="1043" data-end="1133"> <p data-start="1045" data-end="1133">The ICC actively protects its texts because codebook sales are a major revenue source.</p> </li> <li data-start="1137" data-end="1212"> <p data-start="1139" data-end="1212">⚠️ If your app shows exact wording from the IRC, you should license it.</p> </li> </ul> </li> <li data-start="1214" data-end="1449"> <p data-start="1217" data-end="1272"><strong data-start="1217" data-end="1270">If you paraphrase / summarize into your own words</strong></p> <ul data-start="1276" data-end="1449"> <li data-start="1276" data-end="1354"> <p data-start="1278" data-end="1354">You can safely rephrase rules into “plain English” (your “leiga” version).</p> </li> <li data-start="1358" data-end="1449"> <p data-start="1360" data-end="1449">✅ No royalties needed, since you’re not republishing their copyrighted formatting/text.</p> </li> </ul> </li> </ol> <hr data-start="1451" data-end="1454"> <h2 data-start="1456" data-end="1495">🛡️ Safe Strategy for Your Product</h2> <ul data-start="1496" data-end="1885"> <li data-start="1496" data-end="1602"> <p data-start="1498" data-end="1602"><strong data-start="1498" data-end="1512">UI content</strong>: Use paraphrased, simplified, engineer-written explanations (both technical and leiga).</p> </li> <li data-start="1603" data-end="1718"> <p data-start="1605" data-end="1718"><strong data-start="1605" data-end="1625">Backend database</strong>: Encode rules as conditions (if basement + bedroom → need exit). This isn’t copyrightable.</p> </li> <li data-start="1719" data-end="1822"> <p data-start="1721" data-end="1822"><strong data-start="1721" data-end="1744">Reference citations</strong>: You can show “IRC R310.1” as a citation. <em data-start="1787" data-end="1819">Citing section numbers is safe</em>.</p> </li> <li data-start="1823" data-end="1885"> <p data-start="1825" data-end="1885"><strong data-start="1825" data-end="1848">Avoid verbatim text</strong>: Unless you pay for ICC licensing.</p> </li> </ul> <hr data-start="1887" data-end="1890"> <h2 data-start="1892" data-end="1928">⚙️ If you want to be extra safe</h2> <ul data-start="1929" data-end="2134"> <li data-start="1929" data-end="2023"> <p data-start="1931" data-end="2023">Contact <strong data-start="1939" data-end="1972">ICC for a licensing agreement</strong> if you want to include original IRC text in-app.</p> </li> <li data-start="2024" data-end="2134"> <p data-start="2026" data-end="2134">Many software providers (like Plan Analyst) do exactly this: they paraphrase or pay for text reproduction.</p> </li> </ul> <hr data-start="2136" data-end="2139"> <p data-start="2141" data-end="2161">✅ <strong data-start="2143" data-end="2159">Bottom line:</strong></p> <ul data-start="2162" data-end="2443"> <li data-start="2162" data-end="2289"> <p data-start="2164" data-end="2289">You can absolutely build and sell this app without royalties, <strong data-start="2226" data-end="2286">as long as you don’t reproduce the full copyrighted text</strong>.</p> </li> <li data-start="2290" data-end="2356"> <p data-start="2292" data-end="2356">Use your own wording for checklists, and cite section numbers.</p> </li> <li data-start="2357" data-end="2443"> <p data-start="2359" data-end="2443">If you want verbatim IRC text inside your app, you’ll need a license from the ICC.</p> </li> </ul> <hr data-start="2445" data-end="2448"> <p data-start="2450" data-end="2575" data-is-last-node data-is-only-node>Do you want me to check how much ICC typically charges for a <strong data-start="2511" data-end="2529">licensing deal</strong> for software that reproduces their code text?</p></div></div></div></div><div class="flex min-h-[46px] justify-start"><div class="touch:-me-2 touch:-ms-3.5 -ms-2.5 -me-1 flex flex-wrap items-center gap-y-4 p-1 select-none touch:w-[calc(100%+--spacing(3.5))] -mt-1 class="text-token-text-primary w-full focus:outline-none scroll-mt-[calc(var(--header-height)+min(200px,max(70px,20svh)))]" tabindex="-1" dir="auto" data-turn-id="28fc65d1-ac8c-47ff-b915-6131bff87725" data-testid="conversation-turn-13" data-scroll-anchor="false" data-turn="assistant"><h6 class="sr-only">ChatGPT said:</h6><div class="text-base my-auto mx-auto [--thread-content-margin:--spacing(4)] thread-sm:[--thread-content-margin:--spacing(6)] thread-lg:[--thread-content-margin:--spacing(16)] px-(--thread-content-margin)"><div class="[--thread-content-max-width:40rem] thread-sm:[--thread-content-max-width:40rem] thread-lg:[--thread-content-max-width:48rem] mx-auto max-w-(--thread-content-max-width) flex-1 group/turn-messages focus-visible:outline-hidden relative flex w-full min-w-0 flex-col agent-turn" tabindex="-1"><div class="flex max-w-full flex-col grow"><div data-message-author-role="assistant" data-message-id="28fc65d1-ac8c-47ff-b915-6131bff87725" dir="auto" class="min-h-8 text-message relative flex w-full flex-col items-end gap-2 text-start break-words whitespace-normal [.text-message+&amp;]:mt-5" data-message-model-slug="gpt-5"><div class="flex w-full flex-col gap-1 empty:hidden first:pt-[3px]"><div class="markdown prose dark:prose-invert w-full break-words dark markdown-new-styling"><p data-start="0" data-end="104">Great — let’s look at this from both the <strong data-start="41" data-end="56">opportunity</strong> and <strong data-start="61" data-end="69">risk</strong> angles, in plain business terms.</p> <hr data-start="106" data-end="109"> <h2 data-start="111" data-end="133">📈 Market Potential</h2> <h3 data-start="135" data-end="158">1. <strong data-start="142" data-end="158">Target users</strong></h3> <ul data-start="159" data-end="453"> <li data-start="159" data-end="240"> <p data-start="161" data-end="240"><strong data-start="161" data-end="187">Contractors / builders</strong> → who need to check compliance before inspections.</p> </li> <li data-start="241" data-end="311"> <p data-start="243" data-end="311"><strong data-start="243" data-end="269">Architects &amp; engineers</strong> → who want quick validation of designs.</p> </li> <li data-start="312" data-end="384"> <p data-start="314" data-end="384"><strong data-start="314" data-end="345">Homeowners / DIY renovators</strong> → especially for permits in the U.S.</p> </li> <li data-start="385" data-end="453"> <p data-start="387" data-end="453"><strong data-start="387" data-end="411">Municipal inspectors</strong> → who could use it as a pre-check tool.</p> </li> </ul> <h3 data-start="455" data-end="476">2. <strong data-start="462" data-end="476">Pain point</strong></h3> <ul data-start="477" data-end="715"> <li data-start="477" data-end="528"> <p data-start="479" data-end="528">The IRC is <strong data-start="490" data-end="506">1,100+ pages</strong> and very technical.</p> </li> <li data-start="529" data-end="581"> <p data-start="531" data-end="581">Most professionals don’t have time to search it.</p> </li> <li data-start="582" data-end="650"> <p data-start="584" data-end="650">Mistakes = costly rework, delays in permits, failed inspections.</p> </li> <li data-start="651" data-end="715"> <p data-start="653" data-end="715">A smart checklist tool saves <strong data-start="682" data-end="712">time, money, and headaches</strong>.</p> </li> </ul> <h3 data-start="717" data-end="739">3. <strong data-start="724" data-end="739">Market size</strong></h3> <ul data-start="740" data-end="986"> <li data-start="740" data-end="822"> <p data-start="742" data-end="822">The U.S. residential construction market is <strong data-start="786" data-end="819">trillions of dollars annually</strong>.</p> </li> <li data-start="823" data-end="889"> <p data-start="825" data-end="889">Even a niche tool can carve out a <strong data-start="859" data-end="878">profitable SaaS</strong> segment.</p> </li> <li data-start="890" data-end="986"> <p data-start="892" data-end="986">There’s precedent: tools like <em data-start="922" data-end="936">Plan Analyst</em> and <em data-start="941" data-end="949">FTQ360</em> already exist, which shows demand.</p> </li> </ul> <h3 data-start="988" data-end="1027">4. <strong data-start="995" data-end="1027">Differentiators of your idea</strong></h3> <ul data-start="1028" data-end="1319"> <li data-start="1028" data-end="1109"> <p data-start="1030" data-end="1109">Dynamic <strong data-start="1038" data-end="1080">questionnaire → personalized checklist</strong> (instead of static forms).</p> </li> <li data-start="1110" data-end="1162"> <p data-start="1112" data-end="1162">Dual language: <strong data-start="1127" data-end="1159">technical vs. layman’s terms</strong>.</p> </li> <li data-start="1163" data-end="1224"> <p data-start="1165" data-end="1224"><strong data-start="1165" data-end="1181">Visual panel</strong> showing which rules apply / don’t apply.</p> </li> <li data-start="1225" data-end="1319"> <p data-start="1227" data-end="1319">Potential expansion to other codes (IBC, NEC, energy codes, even international standards).</p> </li> </ul> <p data-start="1321" data-end="1438">👉 If executed well, this could <strong data-start="1353" data-end="1435">find strong adoption in small–medium contractors, design firms, and homeowners</strong>.</p> <hr data-start="1440" data-end="1443"> <h2 data-start="1445" data-end="1469">⚠️ Risks &amp; Challenges</h2> <h3 data-start="1471" data-end="1499">1. <strong data-start="1478" data-end="1499">Legal / Copyright</strong></h3> <ul data-start="1500" data-end="1659"> <li data-start="1500" data-end="1555"> <p data-start="1502" data-end="1555">As we discussed: you can’t just reproduce IRC text.</p> </li> <li data-start="1556" data-end="1659"> <p data-start="1558" data-end="1659">✅ Mitigation: paraphrase rules and cite section numbers. License only if you want verbatim content.</p> </li> </ul> <h3 data-start="1661" data-end="1688">2. <strong data-start="1668" data-end="1688">Trust &amp; Accuracy</strong></h3> <ul data-start="1689" data-end="1968"> <li data-start="1689" data-end="1756"> <p data-start="1691" data-end="1756">Users will only rely on this if they’re confident it’s correct.</p> </li> <li data-start="1757" data-end="1841"> <p data-start="1759" data-end="1841">Any mistake (missing or wrong requirement) could lead to <strong data-start="1816" data-end="1838">failed inspections</strong>.</p> </li> <li data-start="1842" data-end="1968"> <p data-start="1844" data-end="1968">✅ Mitigation: strong engineering validation, disclaimers (“for guidance, not a legal substitute”), and continuous updates.</p> </li> </ul> <h3 data-start="1970" data-end="1993">3. <strong data-start="1977" data-end="1993">Code Updates</strong></h3> <ul data-start="1994" data-end="2226"> <li data-start="1994" data-end="2054"> <p data-start="1996" data-end="2054">The IRC is updated <strong data-start="2015" data-end="2032">every 3 years</strong> (2021, 2024, etc.).</p> </li> <li data-start="2055" data-end="2107"> <p data-start="2057" data-end="2107">Local jurisdictions often adopt with amendments.</p> </li> <li data-start="2108" data-end="2226"> <p data-start="2110" data-end="2226">✅ Mitigation: commit to ongoing maintenance, and maybe modularize rules so regional differences can be swapped in.</p> </li> </ul> <h3 data-start="2228" data-end="2250">4. <strong data-start="2235" data-end="2250">Competition</strong></h3> <ul data-start="2251" data-end="2539"> <li data-start="2251" data-end="2342"> <p data-start="2253" data-end="2342">Existing tools (Plan Analyst, FTQ360, REScheck, etc.) already serve parts of this need.</p> </li> <li data-start="2343" data-end="2415"> <p data-start="2345" data-end="2415">Big software players (Autodesk, Procore) could add similar features.</p> </li> <li data-start="2416" data-end="2539"> <p data-start="2418" data-end="2539">✅ Mitigation: focus on <strong data-start="2441" data-end="2482">simplicity, cost, and niche usability</strong> (make it accessible to smaller firms and individuals).</p> </li> </ul> <h3 data-start="2541" data-end="2567">5. <strong data-start="2548" data-end="2567">Market adoption</strong></h3> <ul data-start="2568" data-end="2770"> <li data-start="2568" data-end="2617"> <p data-start="2570" data-end="2617">Contractors are often slow to adopt new tech.</p> </li> <li data-start="2618" data-end="2677"> <p data-start="2620" data-end="2677">They like <strong data-start="2630" data-end="2668">simple, cheap, and mobile-friendly</strong> tools.</p> </li> <li data-start="2678" data-end="2770"> <p data-start="2680" data-end="2770">✅ Mitigation: make UI dead simple, mobile-first, and affordable SaaS (ex: $10–30/month).</p> </li> </ul> <hr data-start="2772" data-end="2775"> <h2 data-start="2777" data-end="2798">🚀 Overall Outlook</h2> <ul data-start="2800" data-end="3157"> <li data-start="2800" data-end="2935"> <p data-start="2802" data-end="2935"><strong data-start="2802" data-end="2822">Strong potential</strong>: The problem is real, the market is big, and current tools don’t fully solve it in the same user-friendly way.</p> </li> <li data-start="2936" data-end="3032"> <p data-start="2938" data-end="3032"><strong data-start="2938" data-end="2952">Main risks</strong>: credibility, legal text usage, and convincing traditional builders to adopt.</p> </li> <li data-start="3033" data-end="3157"> <p data-start="3035" data-end="3157"><strong data-start="3035" data-end="3048">Best play</strong>: start small → focus on <strong data-start="3073" data-end="3099">MVP for one code (IRC)</strong>, make it <strong data-start="3109" data-end="3141">super simple and trustworthy</strong>, then expand.</p> </li> </ul> <hr data-start="3159" data-end="3162"> <p data-start="3164" data-end="3331" data-is-last-node data-is-only-node>Would you like me to draft a <strong data-start="3193" data-end="3223">mini go-to-market strategy</strong> (target segment, pricing idea, and rollout steps) so you can test if there’s traction before investing big?</p></div></div></div></div><div class="flex min-h-[46px]
