class="flex w-full flex-col gap-1 empty:hidden first:pt-[3px]"><div class="markdown prose dark:prose-invert w-full break-words dark markdown-new-styling"><p data-start="0" data-end="148">Great question — this is essentially a <strong data-start="39" data-end="63">SWOT-style breakdown</strong> (Strengths, Weaknesses, Opportunities, Threats) for your app idea. Let’s map it out:</p> <hr data-start="150" data-end="153"> <h2 data-start="155" data-end="183">💪 Advantages (Strengths)</h2> <ol data-start="185" data-end="1045"> <li data-start="185" data-end="397"> <p data-start="188" data-end="220"><strong data-start="188" data-end="218">Simplicity &amp; Accessibility</strong></p> <ul data-start="224" data-end="397"> <li data-start="224" data-end="299"> <p data-start="226" data-end="299">Instead of 1,100 pages of technical text, users answer 20–40 questions.</p> </li> <li data-start="303" data-end="397"> <p data-start="305" data-end="397">Dual version of rules (technical vs. layman) makes it usable by both pros and non-experts.</p> </li> </ul> </li> <li data-start="399" data-end="631"> <p data-start="402" data-end="423"><strong data-start="402" data-end="421">Differentiation</strong></p> <ul data-start="427" data-end="631"> <li data-start="427" data-end="543"> <p data-start="429" data-end="543">Existing tools (Plan Analyst, FTQ360, REScheck) don’t provide a <strong data-start="493" data-end="535">dynamic Q&amp;A → checklist + visual panel</strong> flow.</p> </li> <li data-start="547" data-end="631"> <p data-start="549" data-end="631">The <strong data-start="553" data-end="578">color-coded tree view</strong> (applies / doesn’t apply) is unique and intuitive.</p> </li> </ul> </li> <li data-start="633" data-end="778"> <p data-start="636" data-end="653"><strong data-start="636" data-end="651">Niche focus</strong></p> <ul data-start="657" data-end="778"> <li data-start="657" data-end="778"> <p data-start="659" data-end="778">Starting with the IRC (residential) keeps the scope narrow, manageable, and attractive to a huge U.S. market segment.</p> </li> </ul> </li> <li data-start="780" data-end="901"> <p data-start="783" data-end="800"><strong data-start="783" data-end="798">Scalability</strong></p> <ul data-start="804" data-end="901"> <li data-start="804" data-end="901"> <p data-start="806" data-end="901">Once you structure the framework, you can expand to other codes (IBC, NEC, ISO, local codes).</p> </li> </ul> </li> <li data-start="903" data-end="1045"> <p data-start="906" data-end="922"><strong data-start="906" data-end="920">Market gap</strong></p> <ul data-start="926" data-end="1045"> <li data-start="926" data-end="1045"> <p data-start="928" data-end="1045">Smaller contractors and homeowners are underserved — most compliance tools are expensive or designed for big firms.</p> </li> </ul> </li> </ol> <hr data-start="1047" data-end="1050"> <h2 data-start="1052" data-end="1091">⚠️ Weak Points (Internal Challenges)</h2> <ol data-start="1093" data-end="1824"> <li data-start="1093" data-end="1243"> <p data-start="1096" data-end="1122"><strong data-start="1096" data-end="1120">Manual Rule Encoding</strong></p> <ul data-start="1126" data-end="1243"> <li data-start="1126" data-end="1184"> <p data-start="1128" data-end="1184">Building the initial rule database is labor-intensive.</p> </li> <li data-start="1188" data-end="1243"> <p data-start="1190" data-end="1243">If not updated regularly, it can get outdated fast.</p> </li> </ul> </li> <li data-start="1245" data-end="1384"> <p data-start="1248" data-end="1270"><strong data-start="1248" data-end="1268">Credibility Risk</strong></p> <ul data-start="1274" data-end="1384"> <li data-start="1274" data-end="1332"> <p data-start="1276" data-end="1332">If users doubt accuracy, they won’t trust or adopt it.</p> </li> <li data-start="1336" data-end="1384"> <p data-start="1338" data-end="1384">You’ll need strong validation + disclaimers.</p> </li> </ul> </li> <li data-start="1386" data-end="1521"> <p data-start="1389" data-end="1413"><strong data-start="1389" data-end="1411">Maintenance Burden</strong></p> <ul data-start="1417" data-end="1521"> <li data-start="1417" data-end="1471"> <p data-start="1419" data-end="1471">Codes update every 3 years. Local amendments vary.</p> </li> <li data-start="1475" data-end="1521"> <p data-start="1477" data-end="1521">Requires commitment to continuous updates.</p> </li> </ul> </li> <li data-start="1523" data-end="1665"> <p data-start="1526" data-end="1549"><strong data-start="1526" data-end="1547">Legal Sensitivity</strong></p> <ul data-start="1553" data-end="1665"> <li data-start="1553" data-end="1602"> <p data-start="1555" data-end="1602">Must avoid copyright infringement by the ICC.</p> </li> <li data-start="1606" data-end="1665"> <p data-start="1608" data-end="1665">If you want to include exact text, licensing adds cost.</p> </li> </ul> </li> <li data-start="1667" data-end="1824"> <p data-start="1670" data-end="1690"><strong data-start="1670" data-end="1688">Adoption Curve</strong></p> <ul data-start="1694" data-end="1824"> <li data-start="1694" data-end="1748"> <p data-start="1696" data-end="1748">Builders/contractors are often <strong data-start="1727" data-end="1745">tech-resistant</strong>.</p> </li> <li data-start="1752" data-end="1824"> <p data-start="1754" data-end="1824">They need simple UX, offline/mobile support, and affordable pricing.</p> </li> </ul> </li> </ol> <hr data-start="1826" data-end="1829"> <h2 data-start="1831" data-end="1868">🌍 Opportunities (External Upside)</h2> <ol data-start="1870" data-end="2526"> <li data-start="1870" data-end="2031"> <p data-start="1873" data-end="1895"><strong data-start="1873" data-end="1893">Huge Market Size</strong></p> <ul data-start="1899" data-end="2031"> <li data-start="1899" data-end="1961"> <p data-start="1901" data-end="1961">Residential construction in the U.S. = trillions annually.</p> </li> <li data-start="1965" data-end="2031"> <p data-start="1967" data-end="2031">Even a small SaaS niche ($20–50/month per user) can scale big.</p> </li> </ul> </li> <li data-start="2033" data-end="2238"> <p data-start="2036" data-end="2062"><strong data-start="2036" data-end="2060">Expansion Beyond IRC</strong></p> <ul data-start="2066" data-end="2238"> <li data-start="2066" data-end="2161"> <p data-start="2068" data-end="2161">Add commercial codes (IBC), energy codes (IECC), electrical (NEC), international standards.</p> </li> <li data-start="2165" data-end="2238"> <p data-start="2167" data-end="2238">Eventually: global versions (e.g., Brazil’s NBR, Europe’s Eurocodes).</p> </li> </ul> </li> <li data-start="2240" data-end="2395"> <p data-start="2243" data-end="2270"><strong data-start="2243" data-end="2268">Integration Potential</strong></p> <ul data-start="2274" data-end="2395"> <li data-start="2274" data-end="2343"> <p data-start="2276" data-end="2343">Could plug into Autodesk, Procore, BIM tools, permitting systems.</p> </li> <li data-start="2347" data-end="2395"> <p data-start="2349" data-end="2395">Marketplace for custom checklists by region.</p> </li> </ul> </li> <li data-start="2397" data-end="2526"> <p data-start="2400" data-end="2422"><strong data-start="2400" data-end="2420">AI Support Later</strong></p> <ul data-start="2426" data-end="2526"> <li data-start="2426" data-end="2460"> <p data-start="2428" data-end="2460">Start static/manual for trust.</p> </li> <li data-start="2464" data-end="2526"> <p data-start="2466" data-end="2526">Later add AI-powered search, explanation, or auto-updates.</p> </li> </ul> </li> </ol> <hr data-start="2528" data-end="2531"> <h2 data-start="2533" data-end="2563">🚨 Threats (External Risks)</h2> <ol data-start="2565" data-end="3231"> <li data-start="2565" data-end="2735"> <p data-start="2568" data-end="2585"><strong data-start="2568" data-end="2583">Competition</strong></p> <ul data-start="2589" data-end="2735"> <li data-start="2589" data-end="2664"> <p data-start="2591" data-end="2664">Established compliance software vendors could copy the concept quickly.</p> </li> <li data-start="2668" data-end="2735"> <p data-start="2670" data-end="2735">Big players (Autodesk, Trimble) may enter if they see traction.</p> </li> </ul> </li> <li data-start="2737" data-end="2916"> <p data-start="2740" data-end="2763"><strong data-start="2740" data-end="2761">Legal / Liability</strong></p> <ul data-start="2767" data-end="2916"> <li data-start="2767" data-end="2846"> <p data-start="2769" data-end="2846">A user relying on your tool and failing inspection could create legal risk.</p> </li> <li data-start="2850" data-end="2916"> <p data-start="2852" data-end="2916">Strong disclaimers + “guidance only” positioning is necessary.</p> </li> </ul> </li> <li data-start="2918" data-end="3108"> <p data-start="2921" data-end="2946"><strong data-start="2921" data-end="2944">Regulatory Barriers</strong></p> <ul data-start="2950" data-end="3108"> <li data-start="2950" data-end="3018"> <p data-start="2952" data-end="3018">Some jurisdictions may not accept software-generated checklists.</p> </li> <li data-start="3022" data-end="3108"> <p data-start="3024" data-end="3108">You’ll need to market it as an <em data-start="3055" data-end="3060">aid</em>, not a replacement for professional approval.</p> </li> </ul> </li> <li data-start="3110" data-end="3231"> <p data-start="3113" data-end="3137"><strong data-start="3113" data-end="3135">Trust Adoption Gap</strong></p> <ul data-start="3141" data-end="3231"> <li data-start="3141" data-end="3231"> <p data-start="3143" data-end="3231">Getting early users to believe in and adopt a new product in construction can be slow.</p> </li> </ul> </li> </ol> <hr data-start="3233" data-end="3236"> <p data-start="3238" data-end="3257">✅ <strong data-start="3240" data-end="3254">In summary</strong>:</p> <ul data-start="3258" data-end="3631"> <li data-start="3258" data-end="3360"> <p data-start="3260" data-end="3360"><strong data-start="3260" data-end="3282">Your key strengths</strong> = simplicity, unique visual/dual-language approach, huge underserved niche.</p> </li> <li data-start="3361" data-end="3457"> <p data-start="3363" data-end="3457"><strong data-start="3363" data-end="3387">Your key weak points</strong> = high manual setup/maintenance, credibility risk if not validated.</p> </li> <li data-start="3458" data-end="3540"> <p data-start="3460" data-end="3540"><strong data-start="3460" data-end="3477">Opportunities</strong> = expand across codes and integrate into existing workflows.</p> </li> <li data-start="3541" data-end="3631"> <p data-start="3543" data-end="3631"><strong data-start="3543" data-end="3554">Threats</strong> = competition from incumbents, liability concerns, conservative user base.</p> </li> </ul> <hr data-start="3633" data-end="3636"> <p data-start="3638" data-end="3864" data-is-last-node data-is-only-node>Would you like me to also suggest <strong data-start="3672" data-end="3730">concrete strategies to turn weak points into strengths</strong> (e.g., “how to handle credibility risk,” “how to streamline updates”)? That could give you a roadmap to strengthen the business case.</p></div></div></div></div><div class="flex min-h-[46px] justify-start"><div class="touch:-me-2 touch:-ms-3.5 -ms-2.5 -me-1 flex flex-wrap items-center gap-y-4 p-1 select-none touch:w-[calc(100%+--spacing(3.5))] -mt-1 w-[calc(100%+--spacing(2.5))] duration-[1.5s] focus-within:transition-none hover:transition-none class="text-token-text-primary w-full focus:outline-none scroll-mt-[calc(var(--header-height)+min(200px,max(70px,20svh)))]" tabindex="-1" dir="auto" data-turn-id="1e7a56f4-1a35-4498-8016-6a97617889d4" data-testid="conversation-turn-17" data-scroll-anchor="false" data-turn="assistant"><h6 class="sr-only">ChatGPT said:</h6><div class="text-base my-auto mx-auto [--thread-content-margin:--spacing(4)] thread-sm:[--thread-content-margin:--spacing(6)] thread-lg:[--thread-content-margin:--spacing(16)] px-(--thread-content-margin)"><div class="[--thread-content-max-width:40rem] thread-sm:[--thread-content-max-width:40rem] thread-lg:[--thread-content-max-width:48rem] mx-auto max-w-(--thread-content-max-width) flex-1 group/turn-messages focus-visible:outline-hidden relative flex w-full min-w-0 flex-col agent-turn" tabindex="-1"><div class="flex max-w-full flex-col grow"><div data-message-author-role="assistant" data-message-id="1e7a56f4-1a35-4498-8016-6a97617889d4" dir="auto" class="min-h-8 text-message relative flex w-full flex-col items-end gap-2 text-start break-words whitespace-normal [.text-message+&amp;]:mt-5" data-message-model-slug="gpt-5"><div class="flex w-full flex-col gap-1 empty:hidden first:pt-[3px]"><div class="markdown prose dark:prose-invert w-full break-words dark markdown-new-styling"><p data-start="0" data-end="91">Michel, let me answer you as your investor — critical, profit-focused, and reality-based.</p> <hr data-start="93" data-end="96"> <h2 data-start="98" data-end="131">📈 Market Potential for a SaaS</h2> <p data-start="132" data-end="465">Yes, there’s a <em data-start="147" data-end="153">real</em> problem here: compliance with the IRC and related codes is messy, time-consuming, and expensive when mistakes happen. An app that cuts 1,100 pages into 20–40 guided questions has <strong data-start="333" data-end="352">practical value</strong>. The market (U.S. residential construction) is massive. Even a small subscription footprint can be profitable.</p> <p data-start="467" data-end="575">However, <em data-start="476" data-end="572">the question is not if it’s useful — it’s if you can build a profitable SaaS company around it</em>.</p> <hr data-start="577" data-end="580"> <h2 data-start="582" data-end="616">💪 Potential Strengths for SaaS</h2> <ul data-start="617" data-end="1059"> <li data-start="617" data-end="702"> <p data-start="619" data-end="702"><strong data-start="619" data-end="639">Clear pain point</strong>: contractors and architects <em data-start="668" data-end="674">hate</em> wading through codebooks.</p> </li> <li data-start="703" data-end="812"> <p data-start="705" data-end="812"><strong data-start="705" data-end="718">Broad TAM</strong> (total addressable market): every single new house, remodel, or permit requires compliance.</p> </li> <li data-start="813" data-end="923"> <p data-start="815" data-end="923"><strong data-start="815" data-end="838">SaaS-friendly model</strong>: recurring need (permits, remodels, updates every 3 years with new code editions).</p> </li> <li data-start="924" data-end="1059"> <p data-start="926" data-end="1059"><strong data-start="926" data-end="951">Room to differentiate</strong>: existing tools are either expensive, inspector-oriented, or very narrow (e.g. REScheck for energy only).</p> </li> </ul> <hr data-start="1061" data-end="1064"> <h2 data-start="1066" data-end="1091">⚠️ Risks &amp; Weak Points</h2> <ol data-start="1092" data-end="1812"> <li data-start="1092" data-end="1259"> <p data-start="1095" data-end="1259"><strong data-start="1095" data-end="1107">Adoption</strong>: small contractors are cost-sensitive and tech-resistant. If this isn’t dead simple and cheap (mobile-first, maybe even freemium), uptake will stall.</p> </li> <li data-start="1260" data-end="1386"> <p data-start="1263" data-end="1386"><strong data-start="1263" data-end="1280">Trust barrier</strong>: one wrong checklist → failed inspection → lost trust. You’ll need <em data-start="1348" data-end="1383">engineer validation + disclaimers</em>.</p> </li> <li data-start="1387" data-end="1511"> <p data-start="1390" data-end="1511"><strong data-start="1390" data-end="1406">Code updates</strong>: every 3 years new IRC editions roll out; plus local amendments. That’s an ongoing cost and liability.</p> </li> <li data-start="1512" data-end="1624"> <p data-start="1515" data-end="1624"><strong data-start="1515" data-end="1530">Competition</strong>: incumbents like Plan Analyst, FTQ360, and even Autodesk could build similar features fast.</p> </li> <li data-start="1625" data-end="1812"> <p data-start="1628" data-end="1812"><strong data-start="1628" data-end="1640">Legal/IP</strong>: ICC owns the code text. You can use the rules but not verbatim text without licensing. You’ll have to paraphrase — or pay royalties if you want to reproduce exact text.</p> </li> </ol> <hr data-start="1814" data-end="1817"> <h2 data-start="1819" data-end="1841">💰 Business Reality</h2> <ul data-start="1842" data-end="2270"> <li data-start="1842" data-end="2006"> <p data-start="1844" data-end="2006"><strong data-start="1844" data-end="1862">Unit economics</strong>: if you sell to contractors, you’re looking at $10–30/month pricing to get adoption. That means you need thousands of users to be meaningful.</p> </li> <li data-start="2007" data-end="2168"> <p data-start="2009" data-end="2168"><strong data-start="2009" data-end="2024">Better path</strong>: selling to <strong data-start="2037" data-end="2077">small architecture/engineering firms</strong> or even <strong data-start="2086" data-end="2104">municipalities</strong> (B2B SaaS) could yield higher ARPU ($200–500/month per firm).</p> </li> <li data-start="2169" data-end="2270"> <p data-start="2171" data-end="2270"><strong data-start="2171" data-end="2193">Upsell opportunity</strong>: integrations with permit systems, BIM, or expansion to IBC/NEC/ISO codes.</p> </li> </ul> <hr data-start="2272" data-end="2275"> <h2 data-start="2277" data-end="2290">🚦 Verdict</h2> <ul data-start="2291" data-end="2957"> <li data-start="2291" data-end="2696"> <p data-start="2293" data-end="2362"><strong data-start="2293" data-end="2320">Yes, there is potential</strong>, but only if you are disciplined about:</p> <ul data-start="2365" data-end="2696"> <li data-start="2365" data-end="2464"> <p data-start="2367" data-end="2464">Starting <strong data-start="2376" data-end="2390">very niche</strong> (just IRC, or even just top 50 rules that fail inspections most often).</p> </li> <li data-start="2467" data-end="2516"> <p data-start="2469" data-end="2516">Keeping UX <strong data-start="2480" data-end="2513">ultra-simple and mobile-first</strong>.</p> </li> <li data-start="2519" data-end="2587"> <p data-start="2521" data-end="2587">Positioning as <strong data-start="2536" data-end="2563">an aid, not a guarantee</strong> (to avoid liability).</p> </li> <li data-start="2590" data-end="2696"> <p data-start="2592" data-end="2696">Building credibility via <strong data-start="2617" data-end="2640">engineer validation</strong> and maybe partnerships with inspection/training orgs.</p> </li> </ul> </li> <li data-start="2698" data-end="2957"> <p data-start="2700" data-end="2725"><strong data-start="2700" data-end="2722">Profitability path</strong>:</p> <ul data-start="2728" data-end="2957"> <li data-start="2728" data-end="2775"> <p data-start="2730" data-end="2775">MVP for contractors (low price, wide base).</p> </li> <li data-start="2778" data-end="2881"> <p data-start="2780" data-end="2881">Then move upmarket into engineering firms / municipalities (higher price, fewer clients, stickier).</p> </li> <li data-start="2884" data-end="2957"> <p data-start="2886" data-end="2957">Only later expand to full RegTech platform (IBC, ISO, other regions).</p> </li> </ul> </li> </ul> <hr data-start="2959" data-end="2962"> <p data-start="2964" data-end="3256">👉 If you want this to become a <strong data-start="2996" data-end="3019">solid SaaS business</strong>, the key question isn’t <em data-start="3044" data-end="3062">can we build it?</em> — it’s <em data-start="3070" data-end="3099">how do we get distribution?</em> Contractors don’t hang out on Product Hunt. You’d need partnerships (e.g., permit software providers, contractor associations, inspection firms) to scale.</p> <hr data-start="3258" data-end="3261"> <p data-start="3263" data-end="3499" data-is-last-node data-is-only-node>Would you like me to run a <strong data-start="3290" data-end="3311">scenario analysis</strong>: what revenues look like if you target (a) small contractors vs. (b) engineering firms vs. (c) municipalities? That would give us numbers to compare which go-to-market is most profitable.</p></div></div></div></div><div class="flex min-h-[46px] justify-start"><div class="touch:-me-2 touch:-ms-3.5 -ms-2.5 -me-1 [.text-message+&amp;]:mt-5" data-message-model-slug="gpt-5"><div class="flex w-full flex-col gap-1 empty:hidden first:pt-[3px]"><div class="markdown prose dark:prose-invert w-full break-words dark markdown-new-styling"><p data-start="0" data-end="175">Michel, let me give you a realistic investor’s view on what kind of <strong data-start="68" data-end="103">Monthly Recurring Revenue (MRR)</strong> this project could expect, based on the market and adoption patterns.</p> <hr data-start="177" data-end="180"> <h2 data-start="182" data-end="231">🎯 Step 1: Market Segments &amp; Pricing Potential</h2> <ol data-start="233" data-end="884"> <li data-start="233" data-end="423"> <p data-start="236" data-end="272"><strong data-start="236" data-end="270">Small contractors / remodelers</strong></p> <ul data-start="276" data-end="423"> <li data-start="276" data-end="306"> <p data-start="278" data-end="306">Price sensitivity is high.</p> </li> <li data-start="310" data-end="363"> <p data-start="312" data-end="363">Likely adoption range: <strong data-start="335" data-end="360">$10–30/month per user</strong>.</p> </li> <li data-start="367" data-end="423"> <p data-start="369" data-end="423">This segment is large, but churn could also be high.</p> </li> </ul> </li> <li data-start="425" data-end="663"> <p data-start="428" data-end="479"><strong data-start="428" data-end="477">Small–medium engineering / architecture firms</strong></p> <ul data-start="483" data-end="663"> <li data-start="483" data-end="536"> <p data-start="485" data-end="536">Higher willingness to pay for compliance support.</p> </li> <li data-start="540" data-end="595"> <p data-start="542" data-end="595">Likely adoption range: <strong data-start="565" data-end="592">$100–500/month per firm</strong>.</p> </li> <li data-start="599" data-end="663"> <p data-start="601" data-end="663">Smaller market, but stickier once integrated into workflows.</p> </li> </ul> </li> <li data-start="665" data-end="884"> <p data-start="668" data-end="713"><strong data-start="668" data-end="711">Municipalities / inspection departments</strong></p> <ul data-start="717" data-end="884"> <li data-start="717" data-end="755"> <p data-start="719" data-end="755">Could be sold as enterprise deals.</p> </li> <li data-start="759" data-end="812"> <p data-start="761" data-end="812">Contract value: <strong data-start="777" data-end="809">$5k–30k/year per city/county</strong>.</p> </li> <li data-start="816" data-end="884"> <p data-start="818" data-end="884">Sales cycle much longer, but high credibility if you land a few.</p> </li> </ul> </li> </ol> <hr data-start="886" data-end="889"> <h2 data-start="891" data-end="922">📊 Step 2: Scenarios for MRR</h2> <ul data-start="924" data-end="1462"> <li data-start="924" data-end="1071"> <p data-start="926" data-end="961"><strong data-start="926" data-end="959">Low-end contractor SaaS model</strong></p> <ul data-start="964" data-end="1071"> <li data-start="964" data-end="1008"> <p data-start="966" data-end="1008">1,000 users at $20/month → <strong data-start="993" data-end="1005">$20k MRR</strong>.</p> </li> <li data-start="1011" data-end="1071"> <p data-start="1013" data-end="1071">Would require aggressive distribution and churn control.</p> </li> </ul> </li> <li data-start="1073" data-end="1276"> <p data-start="1075" data-end="1131"><strong data-start="1075" data-end="1109">Mixed contractor + small firms</strong> (balanced scenario)</p> <ul data-start="1134" data-end="1276"> <li data-start="1134" data-end="1196"> <p data-start="1136" data-end="1196">500 contractors at $20 + 100 firms at $250 → <strong data-start="1181" data-end="1193">$45k MRR</strong>.</p> </li> <li data-start="1199" data-end="1276"> <p data-start="1201" data-end="1276">Achievable with 6–12 months of strong marketing and a good referral loop.</p> </li> </ul> </li> <li data-start="1278" data-end="1462"> <p data-start="1280" data-end="1318"><strong data-start="1280" data-end="1316">Enterprise tilt (municipalities)</strong></p> <ul data-start="1321" data-end="1462"> <li data-start="1321" data-end="1382"> <p data-start="1323" data-end="1382">20 municipalities at $1,000/month average → <strong data-start="1367" data-end="1379">$20k MRR</strong>.</p> </li> <li data-start="1385" data-end="1462"> <p data-start="1387" data-end="1462">Combine with smaller accounts and you could reach <strong data-start="1437" data-end="1452">$60–80k MRR</strong> faster.</p> </li> </ul> </li> </ul> <hr data-start="1464" data-end="1467"> <h2 data-start="1469" data-end="1490">⏳ Timeline Reality</h2> <ul data-start="1491" data-end="1850"> <li data-start="1491" data-end="1586"> <p data-start="1493" data-end="1586"><strong data-start="1493" data-end="1511">First 6 months</strong> → very small (sub-$5k MRR), because you’re proving accuracy + usability.</p> </li> <li data-start="1587" data-end="1704"> <p data-start="1589" data-end="1704"><strong data-start="1589" data-end="1602">12 months</strong> → If you nail distribution, credibility, and keep pricing accessible, <strong data-start="1673" data-end="1688">$15–30k MRR</strong> is realistic.</p> </li> <li data-start="1705" data-end="1850"> <p data-start="1707" data-end="1850"><strong data-start="1707" data-end="1720">24 months</strong> → With expansions to other codes (IBC, NEC, IECC), integrations, and B2B/municipality sales, you could target <strong data-start="1831" data-end="1847">$50–100k MRR</strong>.</p> </li> </ul> <hr data-start="1852" data-end="1855"> <h2 data-start="1857" data-end="1890">🚨 Key Risks to Revenue Growth</h2> <ul data-start="1891" data-end="2280"> <li data-start="1891" data-end="2032"> <p data-start="1893" data-end="2032"><strong data-start="1893" data-end="1909">Distribution</strong>: contractors don’t find SaaS tools organically — you’ll need channels (associations, inspector referrals, partnerships).</p> </li> <li data-start="2033" data-end="2136"> <p data-start="2035" data-end="2136"><strong data-start="2035" data-end="2050">Credibility</strong>: a single wrong checklist can tank adoption. You need “engineer-approved” branding.</p> </li> <li data-start="2137" data-end="2280"> <p data-start="2139" data-end="2280"><strong data-start="2139" data-end="2148">Churn</strong>: contractors may only need it when filing permits. You’ll need retention strategies (updates, multi-code coverage, annual plans).</p> </li> </ul> <hr data-start="2282" data-end="2285"> <p data-start="2287" data-end="2356">✅ <strong data-start="2289" data-end="2309">Investor verdict</strong>:<br data-start="2310" data-end="2313"> This can be a <strong data-start="2327" data-end="2346">profitable SaaS</strong> if you:</p> <ul data-start="2357" data-end="2594"> <li data-start="2357" data-end="2397"> <p data-start="2359" data-end="2397">Start narrow (IRC, top pain points).</p> </li> <li data-start="2398" data-end="2455"> <p data-start="2400" data-end="2455">Price smartly (contractors = volume, firms = margin).</p> </li> <li data-start="2456" data-end="2531"> <p data-start="2458" data-end="2531">Land credibility early (engineer endorsements, inspector partnerships).</p> </li> <li data-start="2532" data-end="2594"> <p data-start="2534" data-end="2594">Think about upsell/expansion into other codes and regions.</p> </li> </ul> <p data-start="2596" data-end="2702">Realistically, I’d back a plan where you aim for <strong data-start="2645" data-end="2676">$20–30k MRR in 12–18 months</strong> as a success milestone.</p> <hr data-start="2704" data-end="2707"> <p data-start="2709" data-end="2910" data-is-last-node data-is-only-node>Would you like me to build a <strong data-start="2738" data-end="2795">financial model (with adoption + pricing assumptions)</strong> so you can see how different go-to-market choices (contractors vs. firms vs. municipalities) change the MRR curve?</p></div></div></div></div><div class="flex min-h-[46px] justify-start"><div class="touch:-me-2 touch:-ms-3.5 -ms-2.5 -me-1 flex flex-wrap items-center gap-y-4 p-1 select-none touch:w-[calc(100%+--spacing(3.5))] -mt-1
